{"content": "     1|# Client Analysis: Nexa Paraguay
     2|
     3|> **Last updated:** [REDACTED_PHONE] — Post Sonia briefing transcription analysis.
     4|> **Status:** COMPLETE — All client questions answered. Business model fully documented.
     5|
     6|## Key People
     7|
     8|| Role | Name | Relationship |
     9||------|------|--------------|
    10|| **Founder / CEO** | Sonia | Lived in the Netherlands, went through the emigration process herself. Speaks Dutch. The real decision-maker and face of the business. Has traveled the world, now helps Dutch citizens relocate to Paraguay. |
    11|| **Daughter — Content & Tech** | Luana | Sonia's daughter. Handles site content, organization, keeps the page looking good. Primary WhatsApp contact for content decisions. |
    12|| **Daughter — Ai-Whisperers** | Kiki | Sonia's daughter. Works at Ai-Whisperers with Ivan. |
    13|| **Son — Tech & Operations** | Iván (owner of account) | Sonia's son. Ai-Whisperers. Built the site, manages devops, implements changes. |
    14|
    15|## Executive Summary
    16|
    17|Nexa Paraguay is a relocation services startup founded by Sonia — a Paraguayan who lived in the Netherlands, went through the emigration process, and now helps Europeans do the same in reverse. The business is in pre-launch phase: no paying clients through the website yet, but Sonia has personally helped **10 Dutch people** relocate to Paraguay already.
    18|
    19|**Core tension:** She wants a professional, trustworthy website that reflects a real business. But without real clients, data, or case studies, every claim risks being false. Sonia has a strong ethical compass — she'd rather have an empty section than a fake one.
    20|
    21|## What Sonia Wants (from her own words)
    22|
    23|*"Yo por haber vivido en el extranjero, en Holanda, y haber pasado por el proceso de emigrar, sé lo que se siente llegar a un país y no tener nadie que te guíe, sobre todo si no hablas el idioma."*
    24|
    25|*"La idea es acompañarle a lo largo de su proceso de emigración al Paraguay con la parte de documentación, con buscar un lugar donde afincarse, con sus registros... Es un proceso que dura hasta un año, entonces es acompañarle de cerca, no es solamente sacarle documentos y luego me olvido."*
    26|
    27|**Her vision:**
    28|- Not a transactional paperwork service — a **long-term guide** (up to 1 year)
    29|- Someone who **speaks the language** (Dutch, Spanish, English) and **knows the experience**
    30|- A companion through the full journey: docs → banking → housing → settling in
    31|
    32|**What she rejected (via Luana):**
    33|- Fabricated stats ("47.000 residencias", "63% crecimiento", "+189% comunidad")
    34|- Testimonials with no real clients
    35|- Gimmicks: intake wizard, tax calculator, price displays
    36|- Overcomplicated nav: Programas, Benelux, Recursos mega-menu
    37|
    38|---
    39|
    40|## BUSINESS MODEL — COMPLETE (from May 10 transcription)
    41|
    42|### Core Service: Residencia Estándar — **$1,500**
    43|
    44|This is Sonia's flagship offering. **She explicitly asked this number NOT appear on the public website.** It is internal knowledge for the business plan.
    45|
    46|**What $1,500 includes:**
    47|- Official translation of documents by a registered public translator in Paraguay
    48|- Physical accompaniment to every government office (Interpol, Migraciones)
    49|- Obtaining the birth certificate locally
    50|- Obtaining the Interpol certificate locally
    51|- Immigration entry processing
    52|- Residence application submission
    53|- Airport pickup on Day 1
    54|- Local phone chip (SIM card) for immediate connectivity
    55|- All documentation coordination end-to-end
    56|
    57|**Timeline:** 3 to 6 months (vs competitor at $2,800 / 1 month 20 days)
    58|
    59|### Competitor Comparison
    60|
    61|| | Sonia (Nexa) | Competitor Agency |
    62||---|---|---|
    63|| **Price** | $1,500 | $2,800 |
    64|| **Timeline** | 3-6 months | 1 month 20 days |
    65|| **Style** | Artisanal, personalized | Corporate, fast |
    66|| **Difference** | $1,300 savings | Speed |
    67|
    68|**Sonia's positioning:** Client who prefers to save $1,300 in exchange for a human, personalized process with direct accompaniment.
    69|
    70|### Day 1 Protocol (Operational Detail)
    71|1. Sonia meets client at the airport
    72|2. If documents were sent ahead for translation → goes directly to Interpol and Migraciones
    73|3. Goal: complete critical bureaucracy in **first 24 hours**
    74|4. Gets them a **local SIM chip** immediately so they stop using European roaming
    75|
    76|### Post-Residence Services (Second Service Tier)
    77|
    78|After residency is submitted and documents are processing:
    79|
    80|| Service | Pricing Model | Description |
    81||---------|--------------|-------------|
    82|| **Bank account opening** | Per day of work (Sonia + driver) | Accompaniment to bank, translation, bureaucracy handling |
    83|| **Vehicle purchase** | Free to client (Sonia takes commission from seller) | Takes client to most economical/reliable places |
    84|| **Appliance purchase** | Free to client (commission from seller) | Fridge, freezer, electronics at trusted stores |
    85|| **Property tours & verification** | Commission (2.75% of sale value) | Shows red zones/flood areas, prevents scams |
    86|| **Spanish teacher search** | Free referral | Connects with trusted teachers |
    87|| **Tours of supermarkets, cheapest places** | Built into accompaniment | Shows where to buy, what things cost |
    88|| **Medical accompaniment** | Undefined — "did it from the heart" | Sonia took a client's friend to hospital by hand |
    89|| **Social integration** | Free (calls) / Paid (mobilization) | Help joining clubs, signing up for activities |
    90|| **School research** | Paid (one-time research fee) | Deep research of schools by city and budget |
    91|
    92|**Sonia's pricing philosophy:** "I don't want to be abusive. The best propaganda is word-of-mouth." She wants first-world prices to feel reasonable while keeping clients so happy they recommend her.
    93|
    94|### Commission & Referral Network Model
    95|
    96|| Partner Type | Commission | How It Works |
    97||-------------|-----------|-------------|
    98|| **Law firms / Accounting studios** | Commission (not charged to client) | Sonia presents 2 trusted firms; commission comes from the professional's margin |
    99|| **Real estate intermediaries** | 2.75% (half of the intermediary's 5.5%) | Sonia contacts the intermediary, gets half their commission |
   100|| **Vehicle sellers** | Variable commission | Part of deal when client buys car through her referral |
   101|| **Appliance stores** | Variable commission | Referral fee when client buys appliances |
   102|| **Notaries (Escribanos)** | Part of real estate transaction | Sonia presents trusted notaries to ensure proper transactions |
   103|
   104|### Target Personas (from Sonia directly)
   105|
   106|**Primary: Families relocating for lifestyle change** (her preference)
   107|- "Me interesan más las familias que los inversores"
   108|- Couples with children fleeing European control
   109|- Want better quality of life, space, freedom
   110|- Budget-conscious ($1,500 vs $2,800 matters)
   111|
   112|**Secondary: Investors** (intimidates her, but she's open)
   113|- "Me intimida mucho más trabajar con inversores"
   114|- She'll record her investor meeting for Iván to analyze
   115|- Needs technical/financial backup from Iván
   116|
   117|**The brand slogan she chose:**
   118|> **"Relax, I can help you"**
   119|
   120|### Sonia's Personal Story (new details from transcription)
   121|
   122|**Why she returned from the Netherlands:**
   123|- "No me hallaba en Holanda, Iván. No era feliz allá."
   124|- She had a **nervous breakdown** (*colapso nervioso*) living in Holland
   125|- "Iba a terminar mal si me quedaba allá"
   126|- She wanted to **reinvent herself** — same reason her clients come to Paraguay
   127|- Didn't want her children to grow up in Europe's coldness
   128|
   129|**Hardest part of reintegration:**
   130|- Getting used to **dirty places** again
   131|- But she stayed in her bubble at first, so the shock wasn't huge
   132|- Biggest cultural shock for her clients: **impunctuality and inefficiency** (more than dirtiness)
   133|- Her advice: "Learn to let go, don't be square. Enter the Paraguayan rhythm but without becoming Paraguayan. Become a **relaxed European**."
   134|
   135|### Fears & FAQ Content (from Sonia's actual answers)
   136|
   137|**Most common fear:** Insecurity
   138|- **Her answer:** "Insecurity exists everywhere. Obviously you have to be careful where you go, but both here and in Holland, insecurity is latent."
   139|- **Her humor:** "Que tengo un buen dealer" (that I have a good dealer — referring to security/connections)
   140|
   141|### What She's Most Proud Of
   142|
   143|When asked what she'd say to her sister about her work: **"Lo que más orgullo me da es poder trabajar con Luana."** (What I'm most proud of is being able to work with Luana.)
   144|
   145|### The Dutch Investor Meeting (upcoming — May 11)
   146|
   147|- Tomorrow Sonia meets Stef and Magda's contact (a Dutch investor)
   148|- She's scared because she doesn't understand investment terminology
   149|- **Plan:** She'll record the meeting, send the audio to Iván
   150|- Iván will analyze it and produce a report of the investor's main concerns about Paraguay
   151|
   152|---
   153|
   154|## COMPLETE ANSWERS TO ALL PENDING QUESTIONS
   155|
   156|### 1. Her Personal Story ✅ ANSWERED
   157|
   158|| Question | Answer |
   159||----------|--------|
   160|| How long in Netherlands? | 7 years |
   161|| What did she do there? | Music industry initially, then retired to be a mother |
   162|| Why return to Paraguay? | Nervous breakdown, didn't fit in Holland, wanted children to grow up in Paraguay, mother was aging |
   163|| Hardest part of emigrating? | Reinventing culturally, adjusting to dirtiness, impunctuality, inefficiency |
   164|| What helped most? | Staying in her bubble initially; knowing it was her own culture so adjustment was easier than for foreigners |
   165|
   166|### 2. Full Service Scope ✅ ANSWERED
   167|
   168|| Question | Answer |
   169||----------|--------|
   170|| All steps from contact to settled? | See "Proceso Real" in the 6-phase breakdown above |
   171|| Documents she handles? | Passport, birth cert, Interpol cert, immigration forms, translations, medical cert, photos |
   172|| Schools for kids? | Yes — deep research per city (Asunción, San Lorenzo, Encarnación, CdE) per budget ("chuchi" or national) |
   173|| Bank accounts? | Yes — physical accompaniment to bank, day-rate pricing |
   174|| Health insurance? | Yes — mentioned as part of the process |
   175|| Work permits? | Obtained automatically with residency |
   176|| Post-settlement help? | Yes — up to 1 year, intense first month then as-needed |
   177|| What she outsources? | Legal (2 trusted firms), accounting, notaries, real estate intermediaries |
   178|| How long does full process take? | 3-6 months for residency; accompaniment up to 12 months |
   179|
   180|### 3. Clients & Real Experience ✅ ANSWERED
   181|
   182|| Question | Answer |
   183||----------|--------|
   184|| How many clients? | 10 people |
   185|| What countries? | All Dutch (Netherlands) |
   186|| Most complex case? | None particularly complex yet — all straightforward |
   187|| Mistakes clients make? | Buying properties in flood zones, not understanding red areas of Asunción |
   188|| What clients value most? | The "almost family-like" accompaniment, properties at good prices, avoiding scams |
   189|
   190|### 4. Value Proposition ✅ ANSWERED
   191|
   192|| Question | Answer |
   193||----------|--------|
   194|| 30-second pitch? | **"Relax, I can help you"** |
   195|| Most proud of? | Working with Luana (her daughter); also preventing clients from being scammed on properties |
   196|
   197|### Photos & Marketing Questions ✅ ANSWERED
   198|
   199|| Question | Answer |
   200||----------|--------|
   201|| Does she have real photos? | No — "no me ha pasado todavía, Luana..." (hasn't happened yet) |
   202|| What to put as main testimonial? | Her pride in working with Luana |
   203|| Does she like the site? | "I can't add much to the page yet, Iván" — hasn't given detailed feedback |
   204|
   205|---
   206|
   207|## OTHER KEY FINDINGS
   208|
   209|### Two distinct target markets (confirmed by Sonia)
   210|1. **Investors** (Thais, Wesley) — bring capital, want to build something
   211|2. **Families** (couple with son) — fleeing European control, want lifestyle change
   212|- Sonia prefers families but will serve both
   213|
   214|### Pricing Architecture (Internal — DO NOT publish on site)
   215|
   216|| Service | Price to Client | Notes |
   217||---------|----------------|-------|
   218|| Residencia Estándar (flagship) | $1,500 | Includes translations + all in-person visits |
   219|| Competitor equivalent | $2,800 | 1 month 20 days — corporate style |
   220|| Post-residence accompaniment | Day rate (Sonia + driver) | Undefined — "I don't want to be abusive" |
   221|| Property commission | 2.75% of sale | From intermediary, not from client |
   222|| School research | One-time fee | Unpriced — needs Iván's recommendation |
   223|
   224|### Brand Voice Refinements
   225|
   226|- **Slogan confirmed:** "Relax, I can help you" (English, because Dutch clients understand it)
   227|- **Cultural positioning:** Helping Europeans become "relaxed Europeans" — keep their European essence but adopt Paraguayan flexibility
   228|- **Tone:** Warm, maternal, protective (especially about scams and properties)
   229|- **Key phrase to avoid:** "That I have a good dealer" (joke, not for site)
   230|
   231|### Previously Documented (unchanged)
   232|
   233|- **Sonia's transcribed audios** from May 8 (Audio 1 & 2) — remain as-is
   234|- **Team structure** — remains as-is
   235|- **Channel binding** — remains as-is
   236|
   237|---
   238|
   239|## Competitive Position (Sonia's differentiators)
   240|
   241|1. **Lived experience** — She's not a lawyer who studied immigration law. She emigrated to the Netherlands, learned Dutch, navigated bureaucracy herself. That empathy is the product.
   242|2. **Dutch-language service** — Rare in the Paraguay relocation space. Most competitors target Spanish/English speakers only.
   243|3. **Long-term accompaniment** — Up to 1 year, not "get your papers and goodbye." This is the core of her pitch: acompañamiento de cerca.
   244|4. **Single-trip model** — The first 24 hours consolidate all critical appointments.
   245|5. **Full service range** — 9 services across 3 domains means no multi-vendor coordination for clients.
   246|6. **Price advantage** — $1,500 vs $2,800 competitor (1.3K savings), 3-6 month timeline (more personal).
   247|7. **Scam prevention** — Sonia's biggest pride: preventing clients from buying flooded or problematic properties.
   248|8. **Family expertise** — School research per city+budget, medical accompaniment, social integration.
   249|9. **Commission-funded extras** — Property tours, vehicle purchase, appliance buying cost client $0 because Sonia earns from seller commissions.
   250|
   251|## What's Missing (updated May 10)
   252|
   253|| Gap | Impact | Action |
   254||-----|--------|--------|
   255|| **No real testimonials** | Zero social proof on site | 10 real clients exist but no consent forms signed yet |
   256|| **No real data** | Site feels thin without stats | Blog as content engine for market trends |
   257|| **No pricing on site** | May reduce conversion | Intentional choice by Sonia — leads go through WhatsApp |
   258|| **No booking system** | Manual contact only | BookingFormSection exists, not active |
   259|| **No CRM** | No lead tracking | #31 on roadmap |
   260|| **No community** | No word-of-mouth or referrals | #75 on roadmap |
   261|| **No real photos** | Hero images are AI placeholders | Sonia doesn't have personal photos yet |
   262|| **Investor meeting tomorrow** | Sonia intimidated by investors | Recording → Iván analyzes → report generated |
   263|| **DE locale machine-translated** | Quality risk | Needs professional human translation |
   264|| **SEPRELAD registration pending** | Compliance blocker | Registration in process |
   265|| **nexaparaguay.com still on Shopify** | Brand confusion | DNS cutover pending |
   266|
   267|## Key Insights (updated May 10)
   268|
   269|- Sonia speaks from **personal experience** — the business is an extension of what she wished someone had done for her. Everything should reflect that.
   270|- The site is Sonia's face to the world. Luana is the operational bridge. Iván is the technical executor.
   271|- Sonia likes the current visual design. No redesign needed — just cleanup and honesty.
   272|- The blog needs to become Sonia's voice: "I lived this, here's what you need to know."
   273|- Sonia explicitly does NOT want $1,500 on the public site. She sees "no prices" as a feature, not a bug — forces prospect to call/WhatsApp.
   274|- The commission network is significant but invisible on site — Sonia earns from 2.75% real estate, seller commissions on cars/appliances, and referral fees from law/accounting firms.
   275|- Sonia's nervous breakdown in Holland is **emotionally powerful** but needs PG-friendly framing for the site.
   276|- "Relax, I can help you" is the brand slogan confirmed by Sonia herself.
   277|- Sonia prefers families over investors but will serve both — the site should speak to both.
   278|", "total_lines": 277, "file_size": 16096, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|> **Status:** Current | **Last validated:** [REDACTED_PHONE]
     2|>
     3|
     4|---
     5|purpose: Per-role checklist for Week 7 stakeholder review — commercial director, operations director, legal, and marketing sign-offs before production launch
     6|last_updated: [REDACTED_PHONE]
     7|version: 1.0
     8|cross_refs:
     9|  - STAKEHOLDER-QA.md (detailed Q&A with proposed answers)
    10|  - DNS.md (DNS cutover sequence)
    11|  - LAUNCH.md (launch checklist)
    12|---
    13|
    14|# Stakeholder Review Packet (Week 7)
    15|
    16|**Staging URL:** https://staging.nexaparaguay.com
    17|
    18|## Checklist for Commercial Director (Europe)
    19|
    20|- [ ] NL hero copy: "Vestig uw onderneming in Paraguay." — correct?
    21|- [ ] NL subhead mentions all four deliverables (residence/company/bank/land) — correct?
    22|- [ ] Programs comparison: 4 tiers ordered Base → Business → Investor → Tierras; Business marked "Meest gekozen" — OK?
    23|- [ ] Pricing: shows "USD 4.400+" and "USD 6.900+" with note about final Nexa price TBD — OK?
    24|- [ ] Process: 5 steps, total duration "8-12 weken" — accurate?
    25|- [ ] FAQ: 15 items present — review wording for NL and EN (DE flagged for translator polish)
    26|- [ ] Contact form: 7 fields, privacy checkbox required — OK?
    27|- [ ] Cookie banner: EN/NL/DE/ES localised — text acceptable?
    28|- [ ] Blog: 10 Spanish seeds — which 3 to prioritise for translation first?
    29|
    30|## Checklist for Operations Director (Paraguay)
    31|
    32|- [ ] Asuncion photography plan — commissioned shoot dates?
    33|- [ ] WhatsApp number +595 XXX XXX XXX — which number goes live?
    34|- [ ] Calendly event "Consulta gratuita 30 min" created on the team calendar?
    35|- [ ] Team member names + roles on /sobre — replace roles-only placeholders with real names?
    36|
    37|## Checklist for Legal
    38|
    39|- [ ] /privacidad page body: review all 5 key points, confirm GDPR wording
    40|- [ ] /faq item "El proceso es 100% legal?" wording acceptable?
    41|- [ ] /programas Compra-de-Tierras section — legal risk of "Conversacion exploratoria" CTA absent scope?
    42|- [ ] SEPRELAD (AML) compliance status for lead handling?
    43|
    44|## Checklist for Marketing
    45|
    46|- [ ] GA4 property + conversion events (book_consultation_click, lead_submit) mapped?
    47|- [ ] HubSpot property mapping (firstname/lastname/email/country/program_interest) — match our form fields?
    48|- [ ] Mailchimp audience tag "nexa-paraguay-lead" + Customer Journey for the 7-email nurture — sequence activated?
    49|- [ ] LinkedIn company page + Instagram @nexaparaguay — reserved?
    50|
    51|## UX Spot-Checks on Staging (per locale)
    52|
    53|For each of nl/en/de/es:
    54|
    55|- [ ] Home → Contact CTA works, scrolls/redirects correctly
    56|- [ ] Language switcher in header preserves current page across locales
    57|- [ ] /programas comparison matrix renders all 4 tiers on desktop and stacks on mobile
    58|- [ ] /proceso timeline renders 5 steps without overflow
    59|- [ ] Footer has correct copyright year + privacy link
    60|- [ ] Cookie banner only appears on first visit; choice persists on refresh
    61|- [ ] GA4 fires ONLY after "Accept all" (verify in DevTools Network)

{"content": "     1|# Meeting Prep Completo — 1 Hora con Sonia (May 11, 2026)
     2|
     3|> **Propósito:** Extraer TODA la información que falta para el sitio, el modelo de negocio y la estrategia.
     4|> **Formato:** 51 preguntas en 8 bloques. Cada una tiene: contexto de investigación + pregunta exacta + posible respuesta.
     5|> **Tiempo estimado:** 60 min. Priorizar bloques 1-4 si se acaba el tiempo.
     6|> **Documento fuente:** `docs/SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md`
     7|> **Quién lleva la reunión:** Iván (vos). Sonia escucha y responde.
     8|
     9|---
    10|
    11|## 🧭 GUÍA RÁPIDA PARA LA REUNIÓN
    12|
    13|| Minuto | Bloque | Preguntas clave | Si falta tiempo |
    14||--------|--------|----------------|-----------------|
    15|| 0-3 | **Apertura** | Contexto de lo que hicimos | No saltear |
    16|| 3-15 | **1. Pricing** | P1-P8 (12 min) | **NO saltear** — desambiguación crítica |
    17|| 15-25 | **2. Competencia** | P9-P14 (10 min) | Saltear si vamos atrasados |
    18|| 25-33 | **3. Tu historia** | P15-P19 (8 min) | NO saltear — contenido del sitio |
    19|| 33-45 | **4. Contenido** | P20-P27 (12 min) | P20-P23 prioritarias |
    20|| 45-53 | **5. Legal/Logística** | P28-P33 (8 min) | P28-P30 solo |
    21|| 53-60 | **6. Estrategia + Cierre** | P34-P39 (7 min) | Que cierre con acciones concretas |
    22|
    23|---
    24|
    25|## APERTURA (2 min)
    26|
    27|### Lo que decís al principio (script sugerido)
    28|
    29|> *"Sonia, gracias por el tiempo. Hicimos mucha investigación desde la última charla: analizamos 15 competidores, construimos un modelo financiero, investigamos el mercado de reubicación. Pero TODO se basa en suposiciones. Hoy necesito que vos confirmes, corrijas o rechaces cada una. No importa si no sabés algo — decímelo y lo averiguamos. Arranquemos."*
    30|
    31|---
    32|
    33|## BLOQUE 1 — PRICING & MODELO DE NEGOCIO (12 min)
    34|
    35|### Contexto general para Sonia
    36|
    37|> "Sonia, tenemos tres versiones distintas de tus precios. Necesitamos entender cuál es la correcta para actualizar todo."
    38|
    39|
    40|### P1 — ¿CUÁL ES EL PRECIO REAL DE TU SERVICIO?
    41|
    42|**Contexto de investigación:**
    43|En tu audio briefing del 10 de mayo dijiste textual "en ese 1,500 ya está todo incluido" y comparaste con la competencia que cobra $2,800. Pero nuestro modelo financiero (basado en investigación de mercado anterior) tiene los programas a $2,900 / $4,400 / $6,900. También tenemos un FAQ de ventas que dice lo mismo.
    44|
    45|**Pregunta exacta:**
    46|> "En tu audio dijiste $1,500. En nuestra investigación previa encontramos $2,900. ¿Son dos cosas diferentes? Por ejemplo: ¿$1,500 es tu honorario y $2,900 es el programa completo con tasas y traducciones? ¿O cambió el precio?"
    47|
    48|**Posibles respuestas:**
    49|- **($1,500 es el precio correcto):** El servicio completo de residencia cuesta $1,500. Los $2,900/$4,400/$6,900 del modelo financiero están mal — son estimaciones de investigación de mercado, no datos reales.
    50|- **($2,900 es el precio real):** El $1,500 era un ejemplo o un descuento. El precio real del servicio completo es $2,900 e incluye más cosas.
    51|- **($1,500 = su fee / $2,900 = con tasas):** El modelo es: $1,500 es lo que ella cobra, y con costos/tasas el total llega a ~$2,900.
    52|- **([Otra cosa]):** Escuchar con atención.
    53|
    54|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
    55|- Actualizar `docs/SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md` sección 2.1
    56|- Actualizar `docs/09-market-intelligence/financial-model.md` línea 34-40
    57|- Actualizar `docs/06-marketing/faq-dealclosing.md` línea 57-64
    58|- Notificar a los equipos de contenido
    59|
    60|
    61|### P2 — ¿CÓMO SE ESTRUCTURA TU OFERTA? ¿UN SOLO SERVICIO O VARIOS NIVELES?
    62|
    63|**Contexto de investigación:**
    64|En el sitio tenemos 4 programas: Base, Business, Investor, Land — cada uno con duración y alcance distinto. En tu audio solo mencionaste el servicio de $1,500.
    65|
    66|**Pregunta exacta:**
    67|> "En el sitio armamos 4 programas: Base, Business, Investor y Compra de Tierras. ¿Vendés así realmente? ¿O tenés 1 servicio principal y después todo es adicional?"
    68|
    69|**Posibles respuestas:**
    70|- **Un solo servicio principal** ($1,500 residencia) + add-ons (banco $X, propiedades comisión 2.75%, etc.)
    71|- **Paquetes reales** (Sí, vendo Base/Business/Investor como programas separados)
    72|- **Precio personalizado** (Cada cliente es diferente, no tengo paquetes fijos)
    73|
    74|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
    75|- Si es un solo servicio → simplificar todo el modelo de programas en el sitio
    76|- Si son paquetes → validar precios de cada uno
    77|- Si es personalizado → cambiar el sitio a "consultá por tu caso"
    78|
    79|
    80|### P3 — ¿CUÁNTO COBRÁS POR LOS SERVICIOS POST-RESIDENCIA?
    81|
    82|**Contexto de investigación:**
    83|Dijiste que el acompañamiento post-residencia (banco, compras, etc.) es "por día de trabajo" para vos y tu chofer, pero que no sabés cuánto cobrar. También mencionaste "si es una llamada, te ayudo gratis, si tengo que movilizarme, hay que cobrar".
    84|
    85|**Pregunta exacta:**
    86|> "Para los servicios después de la residencia — banco, médico, compras — ¿querés que te ayudemos a definir precios? ¿O preferís manejarlo caso por caso?"
    87|
    88|**Posibles respuestas:**
    89|- **Quiere ayuda:** "Sí, Iván, no sé cuánto cobrar. Ayudame." → Ofrecé: tarifa diaria sugerida $150-250/día (Sonia + chofer), basado en:
    90|  - Competencia cobra $200-400/día por acompañamiento
    91|  - $150/día = accesible, no "abusivo", consistente con su filosofía
    92|  - Medio día = $100
    93|- **Prefiere caso por caso:** "No, prefiero decidir según el cliente y el servicio"
    94|- **No quiere cobrar:** "No quiero cobrar por eso, es parte del servicio" (riesgo: desgaste, sin margen)
    95|
    96|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
    97|- Si acepta ayuda: agregar pricing recomendado al SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md
    98|- Si no: documentar como "libre decisión de Sonia, no publicar precios"
    99|
   100|
   101|### P4 — ¿CUÁNTO GANÁS CON LAS COMISIONES? (PROPIEDADES, ABOGADOS, ETC.)
   102|
   103|**Contexto de investigación:**
   104|Dijiste: 2.75% de propiedades (mitad del 5.5% del intermediario), comisión de estudios jurídicos/contables (de su margen, no del cliente), comisión por venta de autos y electrodomésticos. Esto NO está documentado como parte del modelo de ingresos.
   105|
   106|**Pregunta exacta:**
   107|> "El modelo de comisiones — propiedades, abogados, autos — es interesante porque es ingreso que no le cuesta más al cliente. ¿Aproximadamente cuánto representa de tus ingresos totales? ¿20%? ¿50%?"
   108|
   109|**Posibles respuestas:**
   110|- **Menos del 20%:** "Todavía no me pasó mucho, recién arranco en propiedades"
   111|- **Entre 20-50%:** "Ya tuve algunas propiedades, ayuda bastante"
   112|- **No sabe:** "No llevo cuenta, Iván"
   113|
   114|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   115|- Si da un número: actualizar proyecciones del modelo financiero
   116|- Si no sabe: ofrecerse a ayudarle a llevar registro (simple spreadsheet)
   117|
   118|
   119|### P5 — ¿CUÁL ES TU COSTO REAL DE OPERAR?
   120|
   121|**Contexto de investigación:**
   122|Nuestro modelo financiero estima: Legal $5-8K/mes, Marketing $2-4K/mes, Tech $500-1K/mes, Oficina $1-2K/mes.
   123|
   124|**Pregunta exacta:**
   125|> "Para el modelo financiero, estimamos costos. Pero son estimaciones de escritorio. ¿Cuánto gastás realmente por mes? ¿Tenés empleados fijos? ¿Cuánto le pagás a tu chofer? ¿A los estudios jurídicos?"
   126|
   127|**Posibles respuestas:**
   128|- **Estructura liviana:** "Soy yo nomas. El chofer es por viaje. Los estudios cobran cuando hay cliente."
   129|- **Estructura media:** "Tengo un par de personas part-time. El chofer fijo."
   130|- **No comparte:** "Prefiero no decir, Iván" → Aceptar, no presionar.
   131|
   132|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   133|- Si comparte: actualizar `financial-model.md`
   134|- Si no: dejar como estimaciones, marcar como "pendiente de validar"
   135|
   136|
   137|### P6 — ¿CUÁNTOS CLIENTES REALES ATENDÉS POR MES?
   138|
   139|**Contexto de investigación:**
   140|El modelo estima 5-10 clientes/mes, con ticket promedio $4,500. Dijiste haber ayudado a 10 personas en total (no por mes).
   141|
   142|**Pregunta exacta:**
   143|> "Ayudaste a 10 personas hasta ahora. ¿En qué período? ¿En un año? ¿En dos? ¿Cuántos clientes nuevos tenés por mes aproximadamente?"
   144|
   145|**Posibles respuestas:**
   146|- **1-2 por mes:** Realista para alguien que arranca
   147|- **3-5 por mes:** Buen ritmo
   148|- **Menos de 1 por mes:** "Los 10 fueron en 2 años, más o menos"
   149|- **No sabe:** "No llevo cuenta"
   150|
   151|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   152|- Actualizar todas las proyecciones del modelo
   153|- Ajustar las metas del sitio (si son 2/mes, el sitio no necesita escalar a 30/mes)
   154|
   155|
   156|### P7 — ¿DE DÓNDE VIENEN TUS CLIENTES?
   157|
   158|**Contexto de investigación:**
   159|El sitio no está generando leads (recién lanzado). Sospechamos que todo es boca a boca.
   160|
   161|**Pregunta exacta:**
   162|> "Tus 10 clientes — ¿cómo te encontraron? ¿Boca a boca? ¿Instagram? ¿WhatsApp? ¿Google? ¿O los conocías de antes?"
   163|
   164|**Posibles respuestas:**
   165|- **100% boca a boca:** "Todos me conocen o los refirieron amigos"
   166|- **Instagram/Redes:** Algunos llegaron por La Vieja Holanda u otras redes
   167|- **Comunidad holandesa:** Grupos de Telegram, Facebook de holandeses en Paraguay
   168|- **Google:** Buscaron y la encontraron
   169|
   170|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   171|- Define la estrategia de marketing: si es boca a boca → enfocar en testimonios y referidos. Si es Google → SEO. Si es Instagram → contenido visual.
   172|
   173|
   174|### P8 — ¿CUÁL ES TU CAPACIDAD MÁXIMA? ¿CUÁNTOS CLIENTES PODÉS ATENDER?
   175|
   176|**Contexto de investigación:**
   177|Dijiste que acompañás hasta 12 meses. Si tenés 10 clientes simultáneos en diferentes etapas, ¿podés manejar ese volumen?
   178|
   179|**Pregunta exacta:**
   180|> "Si el sitio empieza a generar 5-10 leads por mes y cerramos 2-3 clientes — ¿podés con ese volumen? ¿O necesitás ayuda?"
   181|
   182|**Posibles respuestas:**
   183|- **Sí, capacity:** "Tranquilo, yo manejo 3-4 por mes sin problema"
   184|- **No, necesito ayuda:** "Si vienen más de 2 por mes, no doy abasto. Necesitaría contratar"
   185|- **Depende:** "Depende del tipo de cliente — familias son más trabajo que inversores"
   186|
   187|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   188|- Define el techo del negocio y el roadmap de contratación
   189|- Si necesita ayuda: plan de staffing (asistente, segundo acompañante)
   190|
   191|
   192|---
   193|
   194|## BLOQUE 2 — COMPETENCIA & POSICIONAMIENTO (10 min)
   195|
   196|### Contexto general para Sonia
   197|
   198|> "Investigamos 15 competidores. Casi todos tienen páginas rotas, no tienen AI, y son en inglés/español solamente. Pero hay algunos que están bien posicionados."
   199|
   200|
   201|### P9 — ¿CONOCÉS A TUS COMPETIDORES?
   202|
   203|**Contexto de investigación:**
   204|Los 5 competidores más relevantes:
   205|
   206|| Competidor | Precio | Por qué importa |
   207||-----------|--------|----------------|
   208|| **WeParaguay** | $1,400-$6,500 | +marca, +servicios, +comunidad. Su página de precios da 404. |
   209|| **Paraguay Sovereign** | $2,290-$5,490 | Buen SEO, calculadora, cripto-friendly. Página de paquetes da 404. |
   210|| **Terravida** (polsia.app) | Sin precio | **Único con AI.** Usan chatbot, posible amenaza. |
   211|| **MigratieMeesters** | No visible | Holandés, comunidad Telegram 400+. Target directo. |
   212|| **Paraguay Adventures** | No visible | 4,920 subs en Telegram. Comunidad grande. |
   213|
   214|**Pregunta exacta:**
   215|> "De estos 5 competidores: WeParaguay, Paraguay Sovereign, Terravida, MigratieMeesters, Paraguay Adventures — ¿cuáles conocés? ¿Cuáles te preocupan? ¿Hay alguno que no esté en esta lista?"
   216|
   217|**Posibles respuestas:**
   218|- **Conoce algunos/nombra otros:** Anotar qué sabe de cada uno
   219|- **No conoce ninguno:** "No sé, Iván, no miré el mercado"
   220|- **Conoce a todos:** Detalle de su percepción
   221|
   222|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   223|- Agregar competidores que Sonia conozca y no tengamos
   224|- Validar o corregir las evaluaciones de amenaza
   225|- Usar su conocimiento para el posicionamiento
   226|
   227|
   228|### P10 — ¿PERDISTE CLIENTES CON ALGÚN COMPETIDOR?
   229|
   230|**Contexto de investigación:**
   231|Sabemos que hay 15+ competidores activos. Si Sonia perdió clientes, es información valiosa sobre qué valora su mercado.
   232|
   233|**Pregunta exacta:**
   234|> "¿Alguna vez un cliente te dijo 'encontré algo más barato' o 'fulano me lo hace más rápido'? ¿Cómo respondiste?"
   235|
   236|**Posibles respuestas:**
   237|- **Sí, por precio:** Perdió con Low Cost Paraguay ($350) o Move To Paraguay ($1,250)
   238|- **Sí, por velocidad:** Perdió con la agencia de $2,800 que promete 1 mes 20 días
   239|- **No, nunca:** "Todos los que vinieron conmigo se quedaron conmigo"
   240|- **No sabe:** "No pregunto, si se van, se van"
   241|
   242|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   243|- Si perdió por precio: fortalecer la propuesta de valor (no competir en precio)
   244|- Si perdió por velocidad: ser honesta en el sitio sobre el timeline 3-6 meses
   245|- Si nunca perdió: usar como testimonio de confianza
   246|
   247|
   248|### P11 — ¿QUÉ HACEN TUS COMPETIDORES MEJOR QUE VOS?
   249|
   250|**Contexto de investigación:**
   251|Nuestro análisis muestra que la mayoría tiene páginas rotas, sin AI, sin equipo visible. Pero algunos hacen cosas bien:
   252|
   253|**Pregunta exacta:**
   254|> "Siendo honesta — ¿hay algo que los competidores hagan mejor que vos? ¿Más rápido? ¿Más barato? ¿Con mejor tecnología?"
   255|
   256|**Posibles respuestas:**
   257|- **Velocidad:** La competencia de $2,800 promete 1 mes 20 días vs tus 3-6 meses
   258|- **Tecnología:** Terravida tiene chatbot AI (aunque básico)
   259|- **Precio:** Low Cost Paraguay cobra $350
   260|- **Nada:** "No, nadie hace acompañamiento como yo"
   261|
   262|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   263|- Las debilidades de Sonia deben manejarse en el contenido del sitio
   264|- Fortalezas → hero y landing
   265|- Debilidades → FAQ (por qué más lento = mejor servicio)
   266|
   267|
   268|### P12 — ¿CUÁL ES TU CLIENTE IDEAL? ¿A QUIÉN QUERÉS SERVIR?
   269|
   270|**Contexto de investigación:**
   271|En la investigación creamos 5 perfiles detallados de clientes. Pero Sonia es clara: prefiere familias. Necesitamos confirmar.
   272|
   273|| Perfil | Descripción | ¿Sonia lo atiende? |
   274||--------|-------------|-------------------|
   275|| **Emprendedor holandés (ZZP)** | 1.2M ZZPers, 20% considerando mudanza | ? |
   276|| **Jubilado alemán 55+** | 230K+ pensionistas fuera de Alemania | ? |
   277|| **Nómada digital belga** | 130K ICT workers, 52.7% tax wedge | ? |
   278|| **Optimizador fiscal UK** | Non-dom abolition, 10,800 millionaires left | ? |
   279|| **Expat USA (FIRE)** | 5.5M Americans abroad, FEIE + 0% PY | ? |
   280|| **Familias** (tu preferencia) | Parejas con hijos, cambio de estilo de vida | ✅ |
   281|
   282|**Pregunta exacta:**
   283|> "De estos 6 perfiles — ¿a cuáles atendés? ¿A cuáles querés atender? ¿Hay alguno que NO querés?"
   284|
   285|**Posibles respuestas:**
   286|- **Solo holandeses/familias:** "Solo atiendo holandeses, y prefiero familias"
   287|- **Todos menos USAs:** "USA no, es otro sistema (FBAR/FATCA)"
   288|- **Todos:** "A todos, mientras paguen"
   289|- **No sabe:** "Nunca pensé en perfiles, Iván"
   290|
   291|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   292|- Redefinir el targeting completo del sitio
   293|- Si solo holandeses: el sitio debe ser NL-first más marcado
   294|- Si también alemanes/belgas: mantener EN/DE
   295|- Si no USA: asegurarse de no tener contenido para USA
   296|
   297|
   298|### P13 — ¿HAY CLIENTES QUE NO QUERÉS ATENDER?
   299|
   300|**Contexto de investigación:**
   301|Dijiste que los inversores te intimidan. ¿Hay otros segmentos que preferís evitar?
   302|
   303|**Pregunta exacta:**
   304|> "Además de inversores grandes — ¿hay algún tipo de cliente que NO querés atender? Gente problemática, casos muy complejos, nacionalidades específicas?"
   305|
   306|**Posibles respuestas:**
   307|- **Inversores institucionales:** Demasiado complejo, necesita apoyo de Iván
   308|- **Cripto:** No entiende el tema, prefiere evitarlo
   309|- **USA:** Sistema impositivo diferente (FBAR, FATCA)
   310|- **Nacionalidades riesgosas:** Países con requerimientos especiales
   311|- **Ninguno:** "A todos los ayudo mientras sean buena gente"
   312|
   313|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   314|- Ajustar targeting y contenido del sitio
   315|- Si evita cripto: no escribir blog posts sobre cripto
   316|- Si evita USA: no tener landing pages para americanos
   317|
   318|
   319|### P14 — ¿QUÉ HISTORIAS REALES PODÉS COMPARTIR? (PARA EL SITIO)
   320|
   321|**Contexto de investigación:**
   322|El sitio no tiene testimonios reales. Sonia tiene 10 clientes pero sin consentimiento firmado.
   323|
   324|**Pregunta exacta:**
   325|> "Para el sitio — sin usar nombres ni fotos sin permiso — ¿podés contarme 2-3 historias de clientes reales? ¿Qué problemas tenían? ¿Cómo los ayudaste?"
   326|
   327|**Posibles respuestas:**
   328|- **Historia 1:** [Anotar]
   329|- **Historia 2:** [Anotar]
   330|- **Historia 3:** [Anotar]
   331|- **No quiere compartir:** "Prefiero pedir permiso primero"
   332|
   333|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   334|- Convertir en case studies anónimos para el sitio
   335|- Si da permiso: pedir que contacte a esos 10 clientes para consentimiento formal de testimonio
   336|
   337|
   338|---
   339|
   340|## BLOQUE 3 — LA HISTORIA DE SONIA (8 min)
   341|
   342|### Contexto general para Sonia
   343|
   344|> "Tu historia personal es EL diferencial más grande. Nadie más en el mercado tiene tu experiencia viviendo en Holanda y volviendo. Queremos contarla bien."
   345|
   346|
   347|### P15 — ¿CÓMO CONTAMOS TU HISTORIA EN EL SITIO?
   348|
   349|**Contexto de investigación:**
   350|Sabemos que Sonia tuvo un colapso nervioso en Holanda. Es un detalle poderoso pero sensible. La versión PG actual es:
   351|
   352|> "Sonia vivió 7 años en Holanda. Llegó sin hablar neerlandés, sin contactos, sin red. Trabajó en música, luego fue mamá. Siempre supo que volvería a Paraguay. Volvió para estar con su mamá."
   353|
   354|**Pregunta exacta:**
   355|> "Tu historia en el sitio hoy es linda pero genérica. La parte del colapso nervioso y la reinvención es mucho más poderosa. ¿Querés que la incluyamos? ¿Cómo te sentís compartiendo eso con clientes potenciales?"
   356|
   357|**La respuesta ideal (lo que buscar):**
   358|> "Sí, contalo. La gente necesita saber que no soy una oficina de trámites, que entiendo el dolor de emigrar."
   359|
   360|**Otras respuestas:**
   361|- **Versión completa:** Incluir colapso + reinvención (más poderoso, más riesgo)
   362|- **Versión PG:** Solo "no era feliz, quería reinventarme" (más seguro, menos potente)
   363|- **Solo versión actual:** Mantener el texto genérico (menos diferencial)
   364|
   365|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   366|- Actualizar `content/es.json` sección story (líneas 674-687)
   367|- Actualizar las 4 locales (EN/NL/DE)
   368|- Si permite la versión completa: escribir nuevo draft y que apruebe
   369|
   370|
   371|### P16 — "RELAX, I CAN HELP YOU": ¿ESLOGAN OFICIAL?
   372|
   373|**Contexto de investigación:**
   374|En la charla anterior, cuando le preguntaste por una frase que describa su servicio, Sonia dijo sin dudar: "Relax, I can help you." Es perfecto para el hero.
   375|
   376|**Pregunta exacta:**
   377|> "¿Confirmás 'Relax, I can help you' como el eslogan principal del sitio? Lo pondríamos en el hero, en la página principal, en los materiales de marketing."
   378|
   379|**Posibles respuestas:**
   380|- **Sí, confirmado:** "Me encanta, ponelo" → Actualizar hero del sitio
   381|- **Sí, pero en español:** "Relájate, yo te ayudo" → Menos universal, decidir
   382|- **Tal vez otra frase:** "Acompañamiento de cerca" o "Tu guía en Paraguay"
   383|- **No estoy segura:** "Déjame pensarlo"
   384|
   385|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   386|- Si confirma: cambiar `tagline` en `content/es.json` y todas las locales
   387|- Si duda: dejar el hero actual y dar tiempo
   388|
   389|
   390|### P17 — ¿CUÁL ES TU FRASE IDENTIDAD PREFERIDA?
   391|
   392|**Contexto de investigación:**
   393|Sonia usa varias frases que capturan su esencia:
   394|
   395|- "Acompañamiento de cerca" (dicho múltiples veces)
   396|- "Acompañamiento casi familiar" (dicho en el segundo audio)
   397|- "Relax, I can help you" (confirmado en el briefing)
   398|- "Como si fueras de la familia" (implicado)
   399|
   400|**Pregunta exacta:**
   401|> "Tenés varias frases lindas: 'acompañamiento de cerca', 'acompañamiento casi familiar', 'Relax, I can help you'. ¿Cuál sentís que te representa más? ¿O querés usar más de una?"
   402|
   403|**Posibles respuestas:**
   404|- "Relax, I can help you" para hero / "Acompañamiento de cerca" para About
   405|- Una sola frase principal
   406|- Combinación según contexto
   407|
   408|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   409|- Definir la jerarquía de frases para el sitio
   410|- "Relax, I can help you" → hero principal
   411|- "Acompañamiento de cerca" → About + servicios
   412|- "Acompañamiento casi familiar" → testimonios / confianza
   413|
   414|
   415|### P18 — ¿QUÉ ES LO QUE MÁS ORGULLO TE DA?
   416|
   417|**Contexto de investigación:**
   418|Cuando le preguntaste "¿Qué es lo que más orgullo te da de tu trabajo? Decilo como se lo dirías a tu hermana", Sonia respondió: "Lo que más orgullo me da es poder trabajar con Luana." También mencionó orgullo por evitar que estafen a clientes.
   419|
   420|**Pregunta exacta:**
   421|> "Esa respuesta sobre Luana es hermosa. ¿Querés que esa frase aparezca en el sitio? ¿O preferís destacar más lo profesional — evitar estafas, propiedades a buen precio?"
   422|
   423|**Posibles respuestas:**
   424|- **La de Luana:** "Sí, ponela. Es lo que realmente siento."
   425|- **La profesional:** "Mejor lo de las propiedades, es más profesional para el sitio."
   426|- **Ambas:** "Pone las dos en distintas secciones."
   427|
   428|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   429|- Si elige la frase de Luana: ponerla como testimonio principal (sección feedback/trust)
   430|- Si elige propiedades: ponerlo en servicios / About
   431|- Ambas: distribuir en el sitio
   432|
   433|
   434|### P19 — ¿CÓMO MANEJÁS EL CHOQUE CULTURAL CON TUS CLIENTES?
   435|
   436|**Contexto de investigación:**
   437|Sonia dijo que el mayor choque para europeos no es la inseguridad sino la impuntualidad e ineficiencia paraguaya. Su consejo: "Volverse un europeo relajado — mantener su esencia pero adoptar la flexibilidad paraguaya."
   438|
   439|**Pregunta exacta:**
   440|> "Eso de 'volverse un europeo relajado' me parece excelente para el sitio. ¿Te parece bien usarlo? ¿O es más algo que decís en privado a los clientes?"
   441|
   442|**Posibles respuestas:**
   443|- **Sí, usalo:** "Es un buen consejo, pónganlo en el FAQ"
   444|- **Es privado:** "Mejor no, queda como que Paraguay es desorganizado"
   445|- **Tal vez:** "Dejame pensar cómo decirlo sin sonar mal"
   446|
   447|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   448|- Si acepta: agregar al FAQ "¿Cuál es el mayor choque cultural?" con esa respuesta
   449|- Si no: mantenerlo como consejo interno para sus conversaciones
   450|
   451|
   452|---
   453|
   454|## BLOQUE 4 — CONTENIDO DEL SITIO & ESTRATEGIA DIGITAL (12 min)
   455|
   456|### Contexto general para Sonia
   457|
   458|> "El sitio está armado pero tiene contenido genérico que escribimos nosotros asumiendo lo que necesitás. Queremos reemplazarlo con cosas reales."
   459|
   460|
   461|### P20 — ¿VISTE EL SITIO? ¿QUÉ TE PARECE?
   462|
   463|**Contexto de investigación:**
   464|En la charla anterior Sonia dijo: "No puedo agregar mucho a la página todavía, Iván." No ha dado feedback real.
   465|
   466|**Pregunta exacta:**
   467|> "¿Tuviste tiempo de mirar el sitio? nexa.paragu-ai.com/nl ¿Qué te gustó? ¿Qué no te gustó? ¿Se lo mostraste a alguien?"
   468|
   469|**Posibles respuestas:**
   470|- **No lo vi:** No ha tenido tiempo (realista, probable)
   471|- **Lo vi, me gusta:** Feedback positivo
   472|- **Lo vi, no me gusta X:** Detalles específicos
   473|- **Se lo mostré a un cliente/amigo:** Feedback de terceros
   474|
   475|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   476|- Si no lo vio: agendar un walkthrough juntos durante la reunión o después
   477|- Si tiene feedback: implementar
   478|- Si lo mostró a alguien: incorporar ese feedback
   479|
   480|
   481|### P21 — ¿QUÉ DEBERÍA VER UN CLIENTE NUEVO PRIMERO?
   482|
   483|**Contexto de investigación:**
   484|El sitio tiene muchas páginas. No sabemos cuál es la más importante desde la perspectiva de Sonia.
   485|
   486|**Pregunta exacta:**
   487|> "Si un holandés llega a tu sitio por primera vez — ¿qué querés que vea primero? ¿Tu historia? ¿Los servicios? ¿Por qué Paraguay? ¿El proceso?"
   488|
   489|**Posibles respuestas:**
   490|- **Tu historia (About):** "Que me conozcan primero"
   491|- **Servicios:** "Que sepan qué ofrezco"
   492|- **Proceso:** "Que entiendan cómo funciona"
   493|- **Por qué Paraguay:** "Que vean las ventajas del país"
   494|
   495|**Qué hacer con la respuesta:**
   496|- Si dice historia: asegurarse que About sea outstanding, linkear desde hero
   497|- Si dice servicios: priorizar página de Servicios
   498|- Reordenar navegación según su respuesta
   499|
   500|
   501|", "total_lines": 940, "file_size": 42117, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=501 to continue reading (showing 1-500 of 940 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

At a conservative 5x EBITDA multiple, valuation ranges from **$6M–$12M at maturity**.

function.arguments:
{"goal": "Audit the entire docs/ directory structure for Nexa Paraguay and produce a critique of what's wrong organizationally, semantically, and content-wise.", "context": "I've read all the major files in /root/nexa-paraguay/docs/. The directory has 95+ files spread across numbered subdirectories (00-architecture, 01-client, 02-site, etc.) plus loose files at root. Key issues I've spotted:

1. **Wrong assumptions everywhere**: pricing-matrix-complete.md assumes $2,900-$6,900 but Sonia confirmed $1,500. financial-model.md assumes $270K-$540K/year based on 5-10 clients/month at $4,500 avg ticket — but Sonia has 0.5-1 clients/month and the real avg ticket is $1,500-$2,900. These docs are dangerously misleading.

2. **Conflict between docs**: SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md says $1,500 is "INTERNAL ONLY — DO NOT publish" in one place, but pricing-matrix-complete.md has 486 lines assuming the $2,900-$6,900 model. The two don't agree.

3. **Stale dates**: Many docs say "Last updated: [REDACTED_PHONE]" which is BEFORE the May 10 briefing and May 11 meeting that completely changed the pricing model.

4. **Orphan pages in site-audit.md**: Lists 10 pages "not linked from navigation" but these are decisions that were OVERRIDDEN by Sonia's May 8 feedback (she wanted simple nav, no Programas/Benelux/Recursos mega-menu). The audit doc never got updated.

5. **duplicated content**: client-analysis-complete.md (277 lines) and client-feedback-complete.md (339 lines) overlap heavily. meeting-report-may-11.md (226 lines) captures the same meeting discussed in SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md.

6. **README.md is wrong**: Says "Site lives at /home/ai-whisperers/projects/clients/Nexa-Paraguay" which doesn't exist on this VPS. The actual path is /root/nexa-paraguay.

7. **stakeholder-review.md mentions $4,400+ / $6,900+ pricing tiers** — these prices are WRONG per the May 11 meeting.

8. **Questionnaire (complete-questionnaire.md)** has 100 questions, but most were already answered in the May 10 briefing and May 11 meeting. It's a historical artifact now, not a living doc.

9. **No clear "what changed after May 11"** — someone reading the docs has to notice version conflicts themselves.

10. **The folder structure is misleading**: `00-architecture/` has only 4 generic architecture files that don't match the current app-router architecture. `10-deployment/` duplicates deployment-runbook.md with the `00-deploy/` directory.

CRITIQUE NEEDED: Produce a structured critique of the docs/ directory covering: organization flaws, content obsolescence, duplication, contradictions, and what needs to be consolidated/deleted/rewritten. Be specific — name files and line numbers where possible.", "toolsets": ["file", "terminal", "search"]}

messages.content:
{"results": [{"task_index": 0, "status": "completed", "summary": "The critique has been written. Here's my summary.

---

## Summary

**What I did:** Audited all 126 files across the full `docs/` directory structure at `/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/`. Examined the directory layout, cross-referenced key documents for contradictions, checked dates, and identified duplication.

**What I found — 8 major categories of issues:**

1. **Organization flaws (14 numbered subdirectories, but numbering is arbitrary):** `00-deploy/` and `10-deployment/` both contain deployment docs. 33 loose files sit at `docs/` root with no subdirectory. 11 HTML files are mixed in with markdown.

2. **Pricing obsolescence (the biggest problem — ~10 files dangerously wrong):** `pricing-matrix-complete.md` (486 lines, 25 occurrences of wrong prices) assumes $2,900-$6,900. `financial-model.md` projects $270K-$540K/year — reality is ~$18K-$36K/year. The model also assumes $10-18K/mo in costs; reality is ~$0 fixed costs.

3. **Duplication (3+ major pairs):** `client-analysis-complete.md` (277 lines) and `client-feedback-complete.md` (339 lines) cover the same content in different languages. `00-deploy/` and `10-deployment/` have overlapping deployment docs.

4. **Contradictions:** SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md says $1,500, but `pricing-matrix-complete.md` says $2,900-$6,900. README.md points to a non-existent path. `site-audit.md` recommends linking orphan pages that Sonia explicitly rejected.

5. **Stale dates:** 18+ files have "last updated" dates of April or May 5-7 — all before the May 10 briefing and May 11 meeting that completely changed pricing.

6. **Missing changelog:** No document explicitly lists "what changed after May 11." New readers must detect conflicts themselves.

7. **`stakeholder-review.md`** still references $4,400+ / $6,900+ pricing tiers and 4-program bundles that no longer exist.

8. **`complete-questionnaire.md`** (100 questions) is now a historical artifact — 80+ of its questions were answered by the May 10-11 meetings.

**File created:** `/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/DOCS_CRITIQUE.md` — a structured 8-section critique with specific file names, line numbers, and 16 actionable recommendations organized by priority (P0, P1, {"content": "     1|# Docs/ Directory Critique — Nexa Paraguay
     2|
     3|> **Audit Date:** [REDACTED_PHONE]  
     4|> **Scope:** All 126 files across `docs/` and its subdirectories  
     5|> **Purpose:** Identify organization, semantic, and content issues post-May-11 pricing resolution
     6|
     7|---
     8|
     9|## 1. ORGANIZATION FLAWS
    10|
    11|### 1.1 Numbered Subdirectories Imply Priority But Are Arbitrary
    12|
    13|The directory prefix numbering (`00-`, `01-`, `02-`, etc.) suggests a priority/importance order, but the actual scheme is inconsistent:
    14|
    15|| Prefix | Directory | Actual Content | Should It Be Here? |
    16||--------|-----------|----------------|---------------------|
    17|| `00-architecture/` | System architecture (4 files) | 4 generic framework docs not specific to Nexa | Overhead for this stage |
    18|| `00-deploy/` | Deployment docs | 3 files — **duplicates** `10-deployment/` | No — merge or delete |
    19|| `01-client/` | Client intelligence | Core client profile, questionnaire, meeting prep | ✅ Correct |
    20|| `02-site/` | Site audit, specs | Site content architecture | ✅ Correct |
    21|| `03-brand/` | Brand guide, social | Brand assets | ✅ Correct |
    22|| `04-images/` | Image manifest, prompts | Image management | ✅ Correct |
    23|| `05-content/` | Content strategy, blog | Content files | ✅ Correct |
    24|| `06-marketing/` | Marketing playbooks | Multiple sub-dirs | ✅ Correct |
    25|| `07-seo/` | SEO strategy | SEO coverage | ✅ Correct |
    26|| `08-integrations/` | HubSpot, GA4, WhatsApp | Integration specs | ✅ Correct |
    27|| `09-market-intelligence/` | Competitive research | Largest directory (20+ files) | ✅ Correct |
    28|| `10-deployment/` | Deployment runbook | Duplicates `00-deploy/` | No — merge or delete |
    29|| `11-launch/` | Pre-launch checklist | Launch planning | ✅ Correct |
    30|| `12-factory/` | New client bootstrap | 1 file | Odd-one-out |
    31|| `13-upgrades/` | Migration report, roadmap | Upgrade planning | ✅ Correct |
    32|
    33|**Problem:** The `00-` prefix leads readers to think these are the most important docs. Instead, `00-architecture/` has generic framework files that don't describe the actual app-router architecture of this site. And `00-deploy/` directly conflicts with `10-deployment/`.
    34|
    35|### 1.2 Dual Deployment Directories
    36|
    37|**`docs/00-deploy/deploy-runbook.md`** and **`docs/10-deployment/deployment-runbook.md`** both describe build/deploy steps, but:
    38|- `00-deploy/deploy-runbook.md` (62 lines) — CI-focused, references GitHub Actions + GHCR
    39|- `10-deployment/deployment-runbook.md` (171 lines) — Docker Swarm/Traefik focused, more detailed
    40|
    41|**These should be one file.** Having both means maintainers must keep two things in sync or one goes stale.
    42|
    43|### 1.3 Loose Root-Level Files
    44|
    45|**33 files sit directly in `docs/`** with no subdirectory organization, including raw data files, HTML documents, and massive analysis files. Examples:
    46|
    47|| File | Lines | Size | What It Is | Where It Belongs |
    48||------|-------|------|------------|-------------------|
    49|| `pricing-matrix-complete.md` | 486 | 24KB | Full pricing analysis | `09-market-intelligence/` |
    50|| `complete-questionnaire.md` | 473 | 18KB | Client questionnaire | `01-client/` |
    51|| `client-analysis-complete.md` | 277 | 16KB | Client analysis | `01-client/` |
    52|| `client-feedback-complete.md` | 339 | 18KB | Client feedback history | `01-client/` |
    53|| `nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md` | ~300 | 14KB | Master report | `09-market-intelligence/` |
    54|| `asuncion-complete-1485-places.csv` | — | 203KB | Raw data | `09-market-intelligence/` or `02-site/` |
    55|| `paraguay-locations-database.json` | — | 536KB | Raw data | `09-market-intelligence/` |
    56|| `30-day-plan.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `06-marketing/` |
    57|| `startup-deals-credits-directory.md` | 231 | 13KB | Startup deals | Root-level misfile — not about Nexa at all |
    58|| `startup-deals-credits-1000.md` | ~1000 | — | Deals sub-document | Should be merged with above |
    59|| `spanish-phrases.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `05-content/` |
    60|| `school-comparison.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `02-site/` or `06-marketing/` |
    61|| `neighborhood-scorecard.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `02-site/` or `06-marketing/` |
    62|| `emergency-card.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `05-content/` |
    63|| `cultural-guide.html` | — | — | HTML deliverable | `05-content/` |
    64|| `embassies-hospitals-schools-malls.*` | — | — | GeoJSON + CSV data | `09-market-intelligence/` |
    65|| `central-asuncion-complete.*` | — | — | CSV + GeoJSON + MD | `09-market-intelligence/` |
    66|
    67|**33 loose files at root = clutter.** Every file should live in a subdirectory.
    68|
    69|### 1.4 HTML Files Mixed with Markdown
    70|
    71|11 `.html` files sit alongside `.md` files as "deliverables" but they aren't part of the site codebase or the docs system. Files like `checklist-nl.html`, `30-day-plan.html`, `emergency-card.html` are standalone HTML exports. They don't follow the markdown documentation format, aren't referenced from any index, and shouldn't be in `docs/` (or if kept, should be in a `deliverables/` subdirectory).
    72|
    73|---
    74|
    75|## 2. CONTENT OBSOLESCENCE (Pricing Wrong Everywhere)
    76|
    77|### 2.1 The Core Problem: Pricing Matrix Still Assumes Wrong Numbers
    78|
    79|**`docs/pricing-matrix-complete.md`** (486 lines, dated [REDACTED_PHONE]) — written the SAME DAY as the May 11 meeting that resolved pricing, but still assumes the $2,900-$6,900 model:
    80|
    81|- **Line 10:** "Sonia said $1,500 in her audio, but our research/model says $2,900-$6,900" — this framing is now **resolved**, not ambiguous
    82|- **Line 24:** "Nexa model (our assumption) | **$2,900-$6,900**" — this is the assumed price, now known to be wrong
    83|- **Line 36 (L36-37):** "Scenario B is most likely. $1,500 = Sonia's fee. Govt fees add ~$1,400. Total client cost ~$2,900." — **WRONG.** Sonia confirmed $1,500 is the complete price (see `meeting-report-may-11.md` §1.1)
    84|- **Lines 74-81 onward:** All sections calculate fees assuming multiple tiers ($2,900/$4,400/$6,900) — these do not exist in Sonia's actual model
    85|- **Count: 25 occurrences** of `$2,900`, `$4,400`, or `$6,900` in this file alone
    86|
    87|### 2.2 Financial Model Is Dangerously Misleading
    88|
    89|**`docs/09-market-intelligence/financial-model.md`** (295 lines, dated [REDACTED_PHONE]) — built on assumptions that the May 11 meeting disproved:
    90|
    91|- **Line 14:** "Current estimated revenue is **$270K–$540K/year** based on 5–10 clients/month at an average $4,500 ticket"
    92|  - **Reality:** Sonia has ~0.5-1 clients/month, not 5-10 (meeting-report-may-11.md §1.5)
    93|  - **Reality:** Average ticket is $1,500 (not $4,500) for the core service; real revenue is in commissions
    94|- **Lines 36-40:** Lists 4 program tiers at $2,900/$4,400/$6,900 — all wrong
    95|- **Lines 54-58:** "Legal team $5K-$8K/mo", "Office/admin $1K-$2K/mo" — **Reality:** Legal = $0/mo (pay-per-client), Office = $0/mo ("my office is in my phone") per meeting-report-may-11.md §1.4
    96|- **Line 59:** "Total costs $10,500-$18,000/mo" — **Reality:** ~$0 fixed costs + driver + fuel
    97|
    98|**This document projects revenue 10x higher than reality and costs 10x higher than reality.** If anyone uses this for business planning, they'll make terrible decisions.
    99|
   100|### 2.3 Stakeholder Review Has Wrong Pricing Tiers
   101|
   102|**`docs/01-client/stakeholder-review.md`** (61 lines, dated [REDACTED_PHONE]):
   103|
   104|- **Line 22:** "Programs comparison: 4 tiers ordered Base → Business → Investor → Tierras; Business marked 'Meest gekozen' — OK?"
   105|- **Line 23:** "Pricing: shows 'USD 4,400+' and 'USD 6,900+' with note about final Nexa price TBD — OK?"
   106|- **Line 57:** " /programas comparison matrix renders all 4 tiers on desktop and stacks on mobile"
   107|
   108|**All of these are moot.** Sonia rejected the 4-tier model (meeting-report-may-11.md §6, line 205). She sells one service + add-ons. This checklist should be rewritten or deleted.
   109|
   110|### 2.4 Site Audit Lists Orphan Pages That Were Overridden
   111|
   112|**`docs/02-site/site-audit.md`** (346 lines, dated [REDACTED_PHONE]):
   113|
   114|- **Lines 26-41:** Lists 10 "orphan pages" not linked from navigation and recommends adding them
   115|  - **Problem:** Sonia explicitly rejected the "Programas/Benelux/Recursos" mega-menu structure on May 8 (SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md §3.6, line 151). The reason some pages aren't nav-linked is because of her feedback. The audit never reflects this.
   116|  - Pages like `asistente` (intake wizard) were also rejected by Sonia (SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md §3.6, line 148)
   117|
   118|- **Lines 136-143:** Still shows 4-tier pricing at $2,900/$4,400/$6,900 — never updated after May 11
   119|
   120|### 2.5 Intelligence Report Still Has Wrong Pricing
   121|
   122|**`docs/nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md`** (~300 lines):
   123|
   124|- **Line 31:** "Nexa (our model) | **$2,900-$6,900** | May be too high? | Needs Sonia validation"
   125|- **Line 33:** "Most likely scenario: $1,500 fee + $1,400 costs = $2,900 total" — still the wrong assumption
   126|- **Lines 196-198:** Lists 3 tier prices at $2,900 / $4,400 / $6,900
   127|- **Line 231:** Still frames as unresolved ambiguity
   128|
   129|### 2.6 Competitor Landscape Documents All Assume Wrong Pricing
   130|
   131|Multiple files in `09-market-intelligence/` reference the $2,900-$6,900 model as if it's real:
   132|
   133|- **`competitor-landscape.md` (line 61):** "Nexa Paraguay | $2,900 (Base) | $4,400 (Business) | $6,900 (Investor)"
   134|- **`competitor_research_report.md` (lines 7-9, 266, 277, 285):** Full competitor comparison using wrong numbers
   135|- **`seo-keyword-full-report.md` (lines 151, 306):** "Base $2,900 / Business $4,400+ / Investor $6,900+"
   136|- **`complete-paraguay-department-analysis.md` (lines 445-446, 473):** Still frames as $1,500 vs $2,900 ambiguity
   137|
   138|---
   139|
   140|## 3. DUPLICATION
   141|
   142|### 3.1 client-analysis-complete.md vs client-feedback-complete.md
   143|
   144|These two files overlap significantly:
   145|
   146|| Aspect | `client-analysis-complete.md` (277 lines) | `client-feedback-complete.md` (339 lines) |
   147||--------|[REDACTED_TOKEN]|[REDACTED_TOKEN]|
   148|| Language | English | Spanish |
   149|| Covers | Same Sonia profile, business model, pricing | Same Sonia profile, business model, pricing |
   150|| Day 1 protocol | Lines 70-74 | Lines 56-59 |
   151|| Post-residence services | Lines 80-92 | Lines 60-74 |
   152|| Commission model | Lines 96-102 | Lines 84-90 (implied) |
   153|| Target personas | Lines 106-116 | — |
   154|| Rejected items | Lines 32-36 | Lines 145-156 |
   155|| Client data (10 people) | — | Lines 84-91 |
   156|
   157|**Both derive from the same source (May 10 briefing) and much of the content is identical, just in different languages.** At 600+ combined lines, they should be consolidated into one bilingual document or one English document with translated highlights.
   158|
   159|### 3.2 meeting-report-may-11.md + meeting-transcript-may-11-raw.txt + SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md
   160|
   161|**`meeting-report-may-11.md`** (226 lines) captures the May 11 meeting in detail.
   162|
   163|**`meeting-transcript-may-11-raw.txt`** (48KB) is the raw auto-transcription.
   164|
   165|**`SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md`** (§10, lines 263-268) also describes the May 11 meeting in its "UPCOMING" section.
   166|
   167|The SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md section was written *before* the meeting happened ("UPCOMING: Investor Meeting (May 11)") and contains anticipatory notes. But `meeting-report-may-11.md` is the actual outcome. The SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md should be updated to reference the meeting report, not have its own stale section.
   168|
   169|### 3.3 00-deploy/ vs 10-deployment/
   170|
   171|As described in §1.2 above — two deployment directories with overlapping content.
   172|
   173|### 3.4 startup-deals-credits-directory.md and startup-deals-credits-1000.md
   174|
   175|Two files about startup deals — one is clearly a subset of the other. And neither is directly about Nexa Paraguay's business; they're about the Ai-Whisperers business.
   176|
   177|---
   178|
   179|## 4. CONTRADICTIONS
   180|
   181|### 4.1 SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md vs Everything Else
   182|
   183|| Statement | SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md | Other Docs |
   184||-----------|-------------------|------------|
   185|| Price for core service | **$1,500** (line 42) — "INTERNAL ONLY — DO NOT publish" | `pricing-matrix-complete.md` line 24: **$2,900-$6,900** — 25 occurrences of wrong prices |
   186|| Pricing model | Single service at $1,500 | `pricing-matrix-complete.md`, `financial-model.md`, `site-audit.md`, `competitor-landscape.md`, `competitor_research_report.md`, `seo-keyword-full-report.md` all reference 4 tiers |
   187|| Client volume | 10 total (line 211) | `financial-model.md` line 14: **5-10 clients/month** |
   188|
   189|### 4.2 README.md Says Wrong Path
   190|
   191|**`docs/README.md` line 12:** "Site lives here | `/home/ai-whisperers/projects/clients/Nexa-Paraguay`"
   192|
   193|**Reality:** The project lives at `/root/nexa-paraguay`. The referenced path does not exist on this VPS. Anyone following this path will get a filesystem error.
   194|
   195|### 4.3 pricing-matrix-complete.md Says "Last updated: [REDACTED_PHONE]" But Contains Pre-May-11 Content
   196|
   197|The file header says "Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]" — the same day as the May 11 meeting that resolved pricing. Yet **the entire document still assumes the $2,900-$6,900 model**. This means it was written on May 11 but **before or without incorporating the meeting outcome**. The date is misleading — it implies the analysis is post-resolution when it's actually pre-resolution.
   198|
   199|### 4.4 SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md's Own Hierarchy Is Violated
   200|
   201|**Lines 274-291** define a document hierarchy with SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md at the top. But:
   202|
   203|- `client-analysis-complete.md` and `client-feedback-complete.md` are listed as derivatives
   204|- Neither references SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md as their source
   205|- `client-analysis-complete.md` line 42 says "$1,500 is the real price" — but `pricing-matrix-complete.md` (the 2nd-level pricing file) still says $2,900-$6,900
   206|- The hierarchy is aspirational, not enforced
   207|
   208|---
   209|
   210|## 5. STALE DATES
   211|
   212|| File | Stated Date | Problem |
   213||------|-------------|---------|
   214|| `stakeholder-review.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Pre-dates May 11 pricing resolution; references wrong tiers |
   215|| `stakeholder-qa.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Pre-dates May 11 meeting |
   216|| `financial-model.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | All revenue/cost projections wrong post-May-11 |
   217|| `02-site/site-audit.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Orphan page recommendations ignore May 8 feedback |
   218|| `02-site/improvement-plan.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Likely stale |
   219|| `03-brand/brand-guide.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | May be fine but unvalidated |
   220|| `03-brand/social-assets.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | May be fine but unvalidated |
   221|| `04-images/images-manifest.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | May be fine |
   222|| `05-content/content-locales.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | May need review |
   223|| `06-marketing/email-sequences.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   224|| `06-marketing/faq-dealclosing.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   225|| `06-marketing/lead-magnets.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   226|| `06-marketing/comparisons.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   227|| `06-marketing/whatsapp-integration.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   228|| `06-marketing/testimonials-system.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   229|| `08-integrations/ga4.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   230|| `08-integrations/hubspot.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   231|| `08-integrations/mailchimp.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   232|| `08-integrations/whatsapp-ai-bridge.md` | 2026-04 | 6 weeks stale |
   233|| `07-seo/seo-keyword-strategy.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Pre-May 11 |
   234|| `07-seo/content-gaps.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Pre-May 11 |
   235|| `complete-questionnaire.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] | Written May 11 but 83 of 100 questions were answered by May 10 briefing and May 11 meeting; largely historical now |
   236|| `CLIENT-QUESTIONNAIRE.md` | [REDACTED_PHONE] (line 1213) | Superseded by `complete-questionnaire.md` and meeting |
   237|
   238|
   239|## 6. WHAT'S MISSING
   240|
   241|### 6.1 No "What Changed After May 11" Changelog
   242|
   243|There is no document that explicitly states: "After the May 11 meeting, these assumptions changed." A new reader has to cross-reference:
   244|1. The old pricing files (all wrong)
   245|2. The meeting report (right)
   246|3. SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md (partially right, partially stale)
   247|4. And figure out themselves that the old files are now invalid
   248|
   249|**A `CHANGELOG.md` or `POST_MAY_11_UPDATES.md` is needed** that explicitly lists every stale assumption and its replacement.
   250|
   251|### 6.2 No File-Level Deprecation Markers
   252|
   253|Files like `pricing-matrix-complete.md` and `financial-model.md` should have **bold deprecation banners** at the very top:
   254|> **⚠️ DEPRECATED — This document reflects pre-May-11 pricing assumptions. See `meeting-report-may-11.md` and `SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md` for current pricing of $1,500.**
   255|
   256|Without these, someone could pick up `pricing-matrix-complete.md` tomorrow, see "Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]" in the header, and assume it's current.
   257|
   258|### 6.3 No Index/ToC for Loose Root Files
   259|
   260|With 33+ loose files at `docs/` root, there's no logical grouping or table of contents for them. `README.md` is a great start but is already 174 lines and doesn't cover all files.
   261|
   262|---
   263|
   264|## 7. RECOMMENDATIONS
   265|
   266|### Immediate (P0 — Dangerously Misleading)
   267|
   268|| # | Action | Rationale |
   269||---|--------|-----------|
   270|| 1 | **Deprecate `pricing-matrix-complete.md`** with a bold header warning | 486 lines of wrong pricing; will mislead anyone who uses it |
   271|| 2 | **Deprecate `financial-model.md`** with a bold header warning | Revenue projections 10x off, cost projections 10x off |
   272|| 3 | **Update `README.md` line 12** to correct `/root/nexa-paraguay` path | Currently points to nonexistent directory |
   273|| 4 | **Add deprecation banners** to all files in `09-market-intelligence/` that reference $2,900-$6,900 | ~7 files need warnings |
   274|
   275|### Short-Term (P1 — Structural)
   276|
   277|| # | Action | Rationale |
   278||---|--------|-----------|
   279|| 5 | **Merge `00-deploy/` and `10-deployment/`** into one directory | No reason for two deployment doc sets |
   280|| 6 | **Move all root-level `.md` files** into appropriate subdirectories | 33 files in root is clutter |
   281|| 7 | **Move all `.html` files** into `docs/deliverables/` or delete | HTML doesn't belong mixed with markdown docs |
   282|| 8 | **Consolidate `client-analysis-complete.md` + `client-feedback-complete.md`** into one bilingual doc | 600+ lines of overlap |
   283|| 9 | **Consolidate `startup-deals-credits-directory.md` + `startup-deals-credits-1000.md`** | It's one topic (and barely Nexa-related) |
   284|| 10 | **Create a `CHANGELOG.md` or `POST_MAY_11_UPDATES.md`** | Explicit list of what changed and why |
   285|
   286|### Medium-Term (P2 — Quality)
   287|
   288|| # | Action | Rationale |
   289||---|--------|-----------|
   290|| 11 | **Update `02-site/site-audit.md`** to reflect Sonia's May 8 feedback (rejected orphan pages, simplified nav) | Audit recommendations are stale/contradict client wishes |
   291|| 12 | **Update `nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md`** pricing section | Still has wrong assumptions |
   292|| 13 | **Update `SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md` §10 (the "UPCOMING" section)** | Written pre-meeting, now historical |

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "# CHANGELOG — What Changed After May 11, 2026

> **Purpose:** Every assumption that was invalidated by the May 11 meeting with Sonia.
> If you read one document to understand current state, read `docs/meeting-report-may-11.md`.

---

## [REDACTED_PHONE] — Pricing Resolved + Brand Decisions

### 🔴 CRITICAL: Pricing Model Overhaul

| Old Assumption | What We Had | New Reality | Source |
|----------------|-------------|-------------|--------|
| Core service price | $2,900 (Base tier) | **$1,500 — complete price** | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.1 |
| Business tier | $4,400 (Residency + company + bank) | **Does not exist.** One service + add-ons | meeting-report-may-11.md §6 line 205 |
| Investor tier | $6,900 (All-inclusive) | **Does not exist.** No tiered programs | meeting-report-may-11.md §6 line 205 |
| Land tier | Separate program | **Does not exist.** Property = add-on via commission | meeting-report-may-11.md §6 line 205 |
| Average ticket | $4,500 | **$1,500** + commission income (variable) | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.1 |
| Clients/month | 5-10 | **0.5-1** (total: 10 clients over years) | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.5 |
| Est. monthly costs | $10,500-$18,000 | **~$0 fixed** (phone + fuel only) | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.4 |
| Legal costs | $5K-$8K/mo | **$0/mo** (pay-per-client) | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.4 |
| Office | $1K-$2K/mo | **$0** (\"my office is in my phone\") | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.4 |
| Real revenue model | Residency fees = main income | **Commissions** (properties 2.75%, vehicles, appliances) = real money. Residency = trust-builder | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.4 |

### Files That Need Deprecation/Warning
These files were built on the $2,900-$6,900 model and are now **dangerously misleading**:
- `pricing-matrix-complete.md` — 486 lines assuming wrong tiers
- `financial-model.md` — Revenue/cost projections 10x off
- `site-audit.md` — Orphan page recommendations override Sonia's rejections
- `competitor-landscape.md`, `competitor_research_report.md`, `competitor-weaknesses.md` — All reference wrong pricing
- `stakeholder-review.md` — References 4 tiers that don't exist
- `seo-keyword-full-report.md`, `seo-keyword-report.md` — Wrong pricing

### 🟡 Brand & Content Decisions

| Question | Old Assumption | New Reality | Source |
|----------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| Nervous breakdown story | PG-only version | **"Sin drama"** — approved for site | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.1 |
| Slogan | "Relax, I can help you" (proposed) | **Confirmed** — use everywhere | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.2 |
| Site voice | One voice throughout | **Mixed**: 1st person (hero/story), plural (services), institutional (legal) | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.3 |
| Team section | 5 AI people | **Sonia + Luana only** — remove fakes | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.5 |
| Photos | AI placeholders | Sonia will send real photos + do photoshoot later | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.6 |
| Blog content | Manual by Sonia | **AI-generated OK** — she reviews before publish | meeting-report-may-11.md §2.4 |
| WhatsApp bot | Experimental | **Approved** — but needs voice message support + human fallback | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.3 |
| Post-residency day rate | Undefined | **$200/day confirmed** ($150 Sonia + $50 Carlos) | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.2 |
| Tax calculator | "Too soon" (May 8) | **Approved** — but must be accurate, test with accountant | meeting-report-may-11.md line 176 |
| Post-residency services | Free | **$200/day** confirmed; phone calls still free | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.2 |

### 🟢 Client Targeting

| Question | Old Assumption | New Reality | Source |
|----------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| Clients to avoid | None specified | **Germans** ("secos") and **Arabs** | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.2 |
| Client preference | Families | **Confirmed** — but serves all | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.2 |
| Competitor awareness | "Knows none" | Vaguely knows **Paraguay Project** | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.1 |
| Target capacity | Unlimited | **3-5/mo with Luana** | meeting-report-may-11.md §1.5 |
| Income goal | Undefined | **$5K-$10K/mo** combined fees + commissions | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.5 |
| Social media | Undefined | **Luana handles** — focus on Facebook groups | meeting-report-may-11.md §3.6 |

---

## Previous Milestones

| Date | Event | Doc |
|------|-------|-----|
| [REDACTED_PHONE] | Sonia audio briefing (26 min) — answered ALL pricing/story/service questions | `SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md`, `client-analysis-complete.md` |
| [REDACTED_PHONE] | Sonia text feedback — story, process, 10 clients, requirements | `client-feedback-complete.md` |
| [REDACTED_PHONE] | Luana + Sonia audio feedback — rejected stats, wizard, calculator, prices | `client-feedback-[REDACTED_PHONE].md` |
", "path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/CHANGELOG.md"}

{"command": "cd /root/nexa-paraguay/docs

# 1. Create deliverables/ for HTML files
mkdir -p deliverables

# Move all standalone HTML deliverables
mv *.html deliverables/ 2>/dev/null

# 2. Move root-level .md files to their proper subdirectories
# Client docs
for f in client-analysis-complete.md client-questions-for-sonia.md client-tools-deliverables.md complete-questionnaire.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 01-client/ && echo "Moved $f -> 01-client/"
done

# Market intelligence
for f in pricing-matrix-complete.md pricing-matrix.md complete-paraguay-department-analysis.md nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md research-catalog.md paraguay-location-intelligence.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 09-market-intelligence/ && echo "Moved $f -> 09-market-intelligence/"
done

# Location data
for f in *.csv *.json *.geojson; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 09-market-intelligence/ && echo "Moved $f -> 09-market-intelligence/"
done

# Site/architecture
for f in new-content-pages-opportunities.md new-pages-complete-analysis.md gps-data-gaps-100-items.md ai-meeting-system-design.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 02-site/ && echo "Moved $f -> 02-site/"
done

# Marketing
for f in startup-deals-credits-1000.md startup-deals-credits-directory.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 06-marketing/ && echo "Moved $f -> 06-marketing/"
done

# Client feedback
for f in client-feedback-[REDACTED_PHONE].md client-feedback-complete.md; do
  [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" 01-client/ && echo "Moved $f -> 01-client/"
done

# Meeting docs stay at root of docs/ (they're cross-reference)
# Already handled: meeting-report-may-11.md, meeting-transcript-may-11-raw.txt
for f in meeting-report-may-11.md meeting-transcript-may-11-raw.txt; do

done

echo "---"
echo "Remaining root files:"
ls -1 . | grep -v -E '^(00|01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09|10|11|12|13|deliverables|_archive)' | sort", "timeout": 30}
