{
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.projectparaguay.nl/faq",
      "title": "FAQs 1: Project Paraguay | Emigratie | Wonen & Leven",
      "content": "# FAQs: Project Paraguay | Emigratie | Wonen & Leven

## Verblijfsvergunning Paraguay

### Vereiste documenten
- **Geboorteakte**, **Verklaring Omtrent Gedrag (VOG)**, en **huwelijksakte** (indien getrouwd)
- Alle documenten moeten **voorzien zijn van apostille** – zonder geldige apostille worden documenten niet geaccepteerd
- Geregistreerd partnerschap wordt niet erkend in Paraguay, maar meenemen wordt aangeraden

### Type vergunning & verlenging
- **Eerst tijdelijke verblijfsvergunning** (Residence Temporal)
- Na 2 jaar: verlenging naar **permanente vergunning** (elke 10 jaar vernieuwen)
- Project Paraguay kan verlenging verzorgen

### Verblijfsplicht
- **Tijdelijke vergunning**: bezoek Paraguay **binnen 1 jaar** na uitgifte (ideaal moment voor bankrekening & rijbewijs)
- **Permanente vergunning**: minimaal **eens per 3 jaar** naar Paraguay reizen

### Garantie & risico's
- **Geen 100% garantie** – geen controle over overheid
- Kans op afwijzing is **extreem klein**; partner heeft 37 jaar ervaring, **0% afwijzing** bij cliënten
- **Waarschuwing**: goedkopere aanbieders leveren zelden de residentie/Cédula; meldingen van slechte ervaringen

### Taal & aanwezigheid
- **Spaans niet vereist** – Engelssprekende tolk aanwezig
- **Fysieke aanwezigheid verplicht** – geen remote aanvraag mogelijk

### Kosten & betaling
- **Geen $5.000 storting** meer nodig (wetswijziging)
- **Korting**: kinderen onder 14 jaar: **€1350 all-in**
- **Crypto betaling mogelijk** via USDT (stablecoin), maar contante euro's bieden momenteel meer koopkracht
- **Geen minimale investering, inkomenseis, bedrijfsoprichting of verplichte verblijfsdagen**

### VOG voor buitenlandse ingezetenen
- Als paspoort niet overeenkomt met VOG: aantonen **minimaal 3 jaar** in buitenland (belastingaangifte, inschrijvingsbewijs)
- Aanbevolen: **VOG voor niet-ingezetenen** aanvragen via:
  - Nederland: [Justis - VOG aanvragen](https://www.justis.nl/producten/vog)
  - België: [link](https://www.vlaanderen.be/vog)

### Bankrekening & Cédula
- Bankrekening openen **na ontvangst Cédula** (nationale identiteitskaart)
- Service beschikbaar tegen extra vergoeding
- Aanvraagprocedure in 3 dagen; documenten worden later uitgegeven
- **Niet mogelijk** om voor anderen (gezin, familie) aan te vragen – iedereen moet persoonlijk verschijnen

## Onroerend goed Paraguay

### Waarom investeren?
- **Stabiliteit & veiligheid**: goed beschermde eigendomsrechten, politieke stabiliteit, lage inflatie, sterke economische groei
- **Veilige haven** voor geografische diversificatie

### Vastgoedmarkt voordelen
- Positief economisch klimaat, jonge groeiende bevolking
- **Lage kosten van levensonderhoud**, stabiel financieel systeem
- **Geen beperkingen** voor buitenlandse kopers
- **Lage maar stijgende** vastgoedprijzen

### Bescherming investering
- Volledige begeleiding: zoeken, overeenkomsten, notariële levering
- Samenwerking met betrouwbare notarissen en advocaten
- **Aankoop op afstand mogelijk** – geen reis naar Zuid-Amerika nodig

### Belastingen
- **Huurinkomsten niet-ingezetenen**: 30% bronbelasting op **50% van brutohuur**
- **Ingezetenen**: 10% als belastbaar inkomen ≥ 10x minimumloon
- **Verhuur**: 5% btw
- **Verkoop**: 50% van waardestijging onderworpen aan 10% inkomstenbelasting
- **Geen vermogens-, erf- of schenkingsbelasting**
- Laagste belastingdruk in de regio

### Extra voordelen
- Bezit van vastgoed **verzekert permanente verblijfsvergunning**
- **Naturalisatie mogelijk na 3 jaar**",

{"content": "# Project Paraguay — Competitive Analysis & Learnings

> **Date:** May 11, 2026
> **URL:** https://www.projectparaguay.nl/
> **Competitor to:** Nexa Paraguay
> **Purpose:** Understand everything they do, what we can learn, what we should avoid, and how Sonia can differentiate.

---

## 1. WHO THEY ARE

**Project Paraguay** is a Dutch-led relocation and real estate company targeting **Dutch (and broader European) expats, investors, and freedom-seekers.**

### Key People

| Person | Role | Background |
|--------|------|------------|
| **Jeroen Pols** | Co-founder, face of the brand | Lives in Hohenau (rural Paraguay). Very online personality. Active in "woke resistance" / freedom movement. YouTube presence. |
| **Lester Rietveld** | Co-founder, real estate advisor | Nyenrode Business University background. Century 21 affiliate. Focuses on property investments. |
| **Anne** | Team member | Operations/expedition support |
| **Sid Lukkassen** | Public intellectual, collaborator | Dutch commentator, appears in their YouTube content |
| **Toine Manders** | Tax advisor | Former Dutch politician. Provides tax structing expertise. |
| **Willem Engel** | Associate (controversial) | Dutch activist. Has appeared at their events. |

### Political Positioning (Important)

Project Paraguay leans **heavily into freedom/sovereign movement** ideology:
- "Plan B" = exit strategy from Europe/NWO/Agenda 2030
- Content features Thierry Baudet (Dutch far-right politician), Willem Engel (anti-lockdown activist), Sid Lukkassen
- Posts about "WO III", "dienstplicht" (conscription), "energie lockdowns"
- Language: "vrijheid", "lot in eigen handen" (take fate into own hands), "beperkingen" (limitations)
- VPRO documentary "Wakker in Paraguay" covers this exact community

**This is a key differentiator.** Project Paraguay attracts a specific ideological audience. Sonia does NOT need to compete for that audience — she can position herself as the **neutral, family-oriented alternative.**

---

## 2. PRICING & PRODUCT STRUCTURE

| Product | Price | What It Includes |
|---------|-------|-----------------|
| **Plan A/B Verblijfsvergunning** (standalone) | **€2,800** (~$3,100 USD) | Full residency + Cedula process, document verification, translations, accompaniment |
| **Expeditie Paraguay** (10-day trip WITH residency) | **€5,397** | Flight NOT included. Hotels, meals, transport, excursions (Iguazú, helicopters, asados), residency + Cédula application, SIM card, bank help, Telegram group access |
| **Expeditie Paraguay** (trip WITHOUT residency) | **€3,397** | Same trip, no residency process |
| **Onroerend goed** (real estate) | Commission-based | Property search, negotiation, notarial process. **Lester Rietveld** is a Century 21 agent. |
| **Children discount** | **€1,350** | Kids under 14, all-in for residency |
| **Container transport** | Separate service | Shipping household goods to Paraguay |

### Key Insight: Pricing Comparison

| | Project Paraguay | Sonia (Nexa Paraguay) |
|---|---|---|
| **Residency price** | **€2,800 (~$3,100)** | **$1,500** |
| **What we get for less** | — | More personal touch, family feel, post-residency support |
| **Tour/expedition** | €5,397 (10 days, all-in) | **Does not offer** ❌ |
| **Children pricing** | €1,350 | **Sonia: same price for all ages?** (Needs to confirm) |
| **Payment options** | Credit card, cash, **Bitcoin (USDT)** | Cash, pending WhatsApp bot payments |

### What This Means for Sonia

| Learn | Apply to Nexa |
|-------|-------------|
| Their price is **€2,800** ($3,100) for residency alone | Sonia's **$1,500** is half their price. This is a HUGE competitive advantage. She can say: *"Misma calidad, mitad de precio."* |
| They offer an **all-in expedition** for €5,397 that includes hotels, meals, helicopter, Iguazú | Sonia could offer a **lower-cost, no-frills version**: no helicopter, no fancy hotels, just her + her car + her network. Price: ~$2,500-$3,000 for the complete experience. |
| They charge **€1,350 for children** | Sonia should decide: same price for families (simpler) or tiered? |
| They accept **Bitcoin/stablecoins** | Sonia doesn't need this yet — cash + bank transfer is sufficient for her volume. |

---

## 3. SITE STRUCTURE & PAGES

| Page | What They Cover | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Home** | Hero, mission, call-to-action, featured blog posts, email capture | Simple but effective. Slogan: "Where Freedom Meets Future" |
| **Verblijfsvergunning** (Residency) | Full process, requirements, pricing, FAQ | **Direct page.** Clean. Transparent about what's needed. |
| **Onroerend goed** (Real Estate) | Investment benefits, tax info, market data | Very convincing macro-arguments. 7 accordion panels. |
| **Expeditie Paraguay** | 10-day tour itinerary, pricing, FAQ | Strongest page. Ultra-detailed day-by-day. Massive FOMO. |
| **FAQ** | Residency + real estate questions | **Excellent.** Comprehensive. Anticipates objections. |
| **Plan B** | Lead magnet landing page | **"6 reasons why a Plan B outside Europe is a good idea"** |
| **Waarom Paraguay** (Why Paraguay) | Benefits of the country | Strong macro-arguments. 6 benefits listed. |
| **Contact** | Simple contact form | Minimal. No WhatsApp integration visible. |
| **Blog** | 20+ posts, YouTube embeds, frequent updates | **Very active.** Multiple posts per week. Heavy on political content + tax updates. |
| **404 page** | Has a redirect + ebook download offer | Smart — captures leads even on errors. |

### What They Have That We Don't

| Feature | Project Paraguay | Nexa Paraguay | Should We Add? |
|---------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|
| **Expeditie/group tour** | ✅ 10-day escorted trip | ❌ | 🟡 Sonia could offer informal accompaniment but not a packaged tour |
| **Blog with YouTube** | ✅ 20+ blog posts, YouTube channel with 60+ videos | ✅ 30 posts (generic AI) | 🟢 Match with real content |
| **Lead magnets** | ✅ "Free ebook" on Plan B + "Why Paraguay" | ❌ | ✅ **YES — high priority** |
| **Telegram group** | ✅ Private group for clients | ❌ | 🟡 WhatsApp group might work better for her audience |
| **FAQ page** | ✅ Comprehensive | ❌ (scattered across site) | ✅ **YES — build this** |
| **Rekentool (calculator)** | ✅ Residency visit timing calculator | ❌ (tax calculator planned) | 🟢 Different calculators, both good |
| **Real estate page** | ✅ Dedicated page with Lester's expertise | ❌ (mentioned in services) | 🟢 Commission model means she doesn't need a full RE page |
| **Container transport** | ✅ Shipping household goods | ❌ | ❌ Not relevant for small-format clients |
| **Crypto payments** | ✅ Bitcoin/USDT | ❌ | ❌ Not needed yet |
| **404 page with lead capture** | ✅ Smart | ❌ | ✅ **Easy win — implement** |
| **Delivery policy page** | ✅ Stripe-compliant cancellation/refund policy | ❌ | 🟡 Needed if she processes payments online |

---

## 4. CONTENT STRATEGY — WHAT WORKS FOR THEM

### Blog Content Mix

| Category | % of Posts | Example Topics |
|----------|-----------|----------------|
| **Tax/legal updates** | 30% | Box 3 changes, exit tax, new Paraguay rules |
| **Political/freedom content** | 25% | Thierry Baudet visit, VPRO documentary, Willem Engel, WO III |
| **Community events** | 20% | Expeditions, asados, record-breaking, aftermovies |
| **Practical advice** | 15% | VERBLIJFSVERGUNNING REGELS (most popular post), costs, requirements |
| **Personal stories** | 10% | Jeroen's experiences, client testimonials |

### What We Can Steal (Ethically)

| Tactic | How to Adapt for Nexa |
|--------|----------------------|
| **Comprehensive blog post on residency rules** | ✅ Sonia's version can be SIMPLER, FRIENDLIER, more personal. Not legalistic — "Sonia explains in 5 minutes how residency works". Add a **visual timeline** (Sonia's approach vs corporate approach). |
| **"Rekentool" (calculator)** | ✅ Build the tax calculator Sonia approved. Later add a residency timing calculator like theirs. |
| **FAQ page** | ✅ Sonia has answered 80+ questions across 3 meetings. Compile them into a clear FAQ on the site. |
| **Lead magnets on every page** | ✅ "10 cosas que nadie te contó sobre Paraguay" — put the email capture on every page. |
| **Testimonials slider** | ✅ They have 12 rotating testimonials. Sonia has 10 clients. Get them to write something. |
| **Ask a question → download** pattern | ✅ Every page has: Email → download → nurture sequence. Copy this exactly. |

### What NOT to Copy

| Tactic | Why Avoid It |
|--------|-------------|
| **Political/fear-based messaging** | Sonia is apolitical. Her brand is "relax, I can help you" — not "flee before it's too late." |
| **"Plan B" / "Crisis" framing** | Sonia should position as "A better life" not "An escape route." Positive framing. |
| **Paid expedition model** | Too complex for Sonia. She could offer a "Day 1 companion" experience but not a 10-day group tour. |
| **VPRO documentary controversy** | Project Paraguay is deeply embedded in the "Wakker in Paraguay" controversy. Sonia should stay far from this. |
| **Heavy tax/legal content** | Project Paraguay has Toine Manders (tax lawyer). Sonia doesn't. Her content should be **practical and personal**, not technical. |

---

## 5. WHAT SONIA HAS THAT PROJECT PARAGUAY DOESN'T

| Sonia's Advantage | Why It Matters |
|-------------------|----------------|
| **$1,500 vs €2,800** | **47% cheaper.** This is massive. Put this front and center. |
| **Personal story + reinvention narrative** | Project Paraguay's face is Jeroen Pols (political commentator). Sonia is a mother who reinvented herself. Massively more relatable. |
| **Family focus** | Project Paraguay targets "freedom-seekers" and investors. Sonia targets families, retirees, and people who want a peaceful life. |
| **Post-residency support ($200/day)** | Project Paraguay offers one-time service. Sonia accompanies for up to a year. |
| **Commission-free property help** | Project Paraguay has a Century 21 agent charging commissions to BOTH sides. Sonia's model is "free to client, paid by seller." |
| **Neighborhood intelligence** | **1,485 places with GPS** – no competitor has this. Sonia can hand a client a map of their neighborhood with schools, hospitals, supermarkets, and restaurants. |
| **Real Paraguayan (not expat)** | Sonia LIVED abroad and returned. She's not a Dutch expat living in Paraguay — she's Paraguayan who understands both worlds. |
| **Luana (bilingual daughter)** | Young, fresh face. Can connect with younger clients. Speaks Dutch natively. |
| **WhatsApp bot (soon)** | Automation advantage. Project Paraguay uses Telegram + email. WhatsApp is the dominant platform. |
| **Huaste/Spanish bilingual** | Can serve Latin American and European clients. |
| **Lower cost of operations** | No office, no employees, no expedition logistics. Her overhead is essentially zero. |

---

## 6. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS

### Steal These Immediately (Free, Fast)

| Action | From Project Paraguay | Effort |
|--------|---------------------|--------|
| **Add FAQ page** | They have a comprehensive FAQ on residency + real estate | 1 hour to compile from Sonia's 80+ answered questions |
| **Email capture on every page** | Every page has "Email → download" | 1 hour of dev work |
| **Lead magnet landing page** | "6 reasons for a Plan B" / "Why Paraguay" | 1 hour to write + set up form |
| **Testimonials slider** | 12 rotating testimonials on the residency page | 30 min (waiting on Sonia's clients) |
| **Add pricing to the residency page** | Transparent €2,800 shown directly | 15 min (Sonia confirmed $1,500 OK) |
| **404 page with email capture** | Redirects to ebook download | 2 min to set up |

### Adapt In The Next Sprint

| Action | Why | Effort |
|--------|-----|--------|
| **Blog post: "How residency works in Paraguay in 2026"** | Their most popular post. Ours can be simpler and more personal. | 2 hours |
| **Visual timeline of the residency process** | They explain it in text. Sonia can do a timeline: Day 1 → Week 2 → Month 2 → Month 6 | 1 hour |
| **"Why Sonia" page** | Direct comparison: Project Paraguay vs Nexa. Free comparison without naming names. | 2 hours |
| **Rekentool adaptation** | Their calculator is for residency timing. Sonia's is for taxes. Both are calculators. | 4 hours (planned anyway) |

### Don't Bother With

| Not Needed | Why |
|-----------|-----|
| YouTube channel | Sonia doesn't want to be a content creator. Blog + testimonials are enough. |
| Telegram group | WhatsApp is her platform. |
| Paid expeditions | Too complex, too expensive. She does NOT have the bandwidth for group tours. |
| Container transport | Not relevant. Her clients bring luggage, not households. |
| Political content | Would alienate her target audience (families, retirees). |

---

## 7. DIRECT COMPARISON TABLE

| Dimension | Project Paraguay | Nexa Paraguay (Sonia) |
|-----------|-----------------|----------------------|
| **Language** | Dutch (NL) | Spanish + will localize to ES/EN/NL/DE |
| **Price** | €2,800 (~$3,100) | **$1,500** |
| **Target** | Freedom-seekers, investors, "Plan B" | Families, retirees, peaceful life-seekers |
| **Tone** | Political, freedom-focused | Personal, warm, "relax" |
| **Location** | Hohenau (rural, south) | Asunción/Casa Weiss (urban, central) |
| **Property focus** | Yes (Century 21 affiliate, Lester) | Limited (commission-based, no dedicated agent) |
| **Group tours** | ✅ Expedities (€3,397-€5,397) | ❌ |
| **Post-residency** | Limited (after sales service) | ✅ Strong ($200/day, up to 1 year) |
| **Blog** | Active, political + tax | 30 posts (generic AI, needs overhaul) |
| **Lead magnets** | ✅ Multiple ebooks | ❌ Not yet |
| **FAQ** | ✅ Comprehensive | ❌ Not yet |
| **Video** | YouTube channel, documentaries | ❌ Not yet |
| **Bot** | Telegram group (manual) | ✅ WhatsApp bot (coming) |
| **Location data** | None visible | ✅ 1,485 places with GPS |
| **Payment** | Card, cash, Bitcoin | Cash, bank transfer |
| **Team** | 4+ people + partners | Sonia + Luana + 2 lawyers + Carlos |
| **Overhead** | High (expeditions, marketing, YouTube) | Near zero |

---

## 8. WHAT I WOULD DO WITH THIS INFO

If I were building Nexa Paraguay to compete head-on with Project Paraguay, here's the strategy:

### Positioning
"Elke euro telt. Elk mens telt. Elke stap telt."

Nexa is not a politically-motivated relocation factory. It's a **personally-guided, affordable, family-friendly alternative** to the expensive Dutch freedom-seeker model.

### Tagline that positions against them
"**$1,500. Sin agenda política. Con acompañamiento real.** "

### Site Changes

1. **Homepage:** Bold pricing. "$1,500 — todo incluido. Misma residencia que Project Paraguay, a casi la mitad del precio."
2. **Services page:** Show ALL services. Project Paraguay has one product. Sonia has 30+ services. Overwhelm with value.
3. **FAQ page:** Build from the 80+ answers we have. Add a section "Project Paraguay vs Nexa" without naming names.
4. **Blog:** First real blog post: "Residencia en Paraguay: la guía completa 2026" — target their most popular search term.
5. **Lead magnet:** "Por qué Paraguay (y por qué Sonia)" — the personal alternative.
6. **Testimonials:** Get the Dutch couple's testimonial ASAP. "I chose Sonia over Project Paraguay because..."

| Children | €1,350 each | **TBD — needs to answer** |
| Real estate | Commission (Lester/Century 21) | Commission (2.75%, free to client) |

### Sonia's ADVANTAGES (what they don't have)

| Advantage | Why It Wins |

{"content": "     1|# Project Paraguay — Competitive Analysis & Learnings
     2|
     3|> **Date:** May 11, 2026
     4|> **URL:** https://www.projectparaguay.nl/
     5|> **Competitor to:** Nexa Paraguay
     6|> **Purpose:** Understand everything they do, what we can learn, what we should avoid, and how Sonia can differentiate.
     7|
     8|---
     9|
    10|## 1. WHO THEY ARE
    11|
    12|**Project Paraguay** is a Dutch-led relocation and real estate company targeting **Dutch (and broader European) expats, investors, and freedom-seekers.**
    13|
    14|### Key People
    15|
    16|| Person | Role | Background |
    17||--------|------|------------|
    18|| **Jeroen Pols** | Co-founder, face of the brand | Lives in Hohenau (rural Paraguay). Very online personality. Active in "woke resistance" / freedom movement. YouTube presence. |
    19|| **Lester Rietveld** | Co-founder, real estate advisor | Nyenrode Business University background. Century 21 affiliate. Focuses on property investments. |
    20|| **Anne** | Team member | Operations/expedition support |
    21|| **Sid Lukkassen** | Public intellectual, collaborator | Dutch commentator, appears in their YouTube content |
    22|| **Toine Manders** | Tax advisor | Former Dutch politician. Provides tax structing expertise. |
    23|| **Willem Engel** | Associate (controversial) | Dutch activist. Has appeared at their events. |
    24|
    25|### Political Positioning (Important)
    26|
    27|Project Paraguay leans **heavily into freedom/sovereign movement** ideology:
    28|- "Plan B" = exit strategy from Europe/NWO/Agenda 2030
    29|- Content features Thierry Baudet (Dutch far-right politician), Willem Engel (anti-lockdown activist), Sid Lukkassen
    30|- Posts about "WO III", "dienstplicht" (conscription), "energie lockdowns"
    31|- Language: "vrijheid", "lot in eigen handen" (take fate into own hands), "beperkingen" (limitations)
    32|- VPRO documentary "Wakker in Paraguay" covers this exact community
    33|
    34|**This is a key differentiator.** Project Paraguay attracts a specific ideological audience. Sonia does NOT need to compete for that audience — she can position herself as the **neutral, family-oriented alternative.**
    35|
    36|---
    37|
    38|## 2. PRICING & PRODUCT STRUCTURE
    39|
    40|| Product | Price | What It Includes |
    41||---------|-------|-----------------|
    42|| **Plan A/B Verblijfsvergunning** (standalone) | **€2,800** (~$3,100 USD) | Full residency + Cedula process, document verification, translations, accompaniment |
    43|| **Expeditie Paraguay** (10-day trip WITH residency) | **€5,397** | Flight NOT included. Hotels, meals, transport, excursions (Iguazú, helicopters, asados), residency + Cédula application, SIM card, bank help, Telegram group access |
    44|| **Expeditie Paraguay** (trip WITHOUT residency) | **€3,397** | Same trip, no residency process |
    45|| **Onroerend goed** (real estate) | Commission-based | Property search, negotiation, notarial process. **Lester Rietveld** is a Century 21 agent. |
    46|| **Children discount** | **€1,350** | Kids under 14, all-in for residency |
    47|| **Container transport** | Separate service | Shipping household goods to Paraguay |
    48|
    49|### Key Insight: Pricing Comparison
    50|
    51|| | Project Paraguay | Sonia (Nexa Paraguay) |
    52||---|---|---|
    53|| **Residency price** | **€2,800 (~$3,100)** | **$1,500** |
    54|| **What we get for less** | — | More personal touch, family feel, post-residency support |
    55|| **Tour/expedition** | €5,397 (10 days, all-in) | **Does not offer** ❌ |
    56|| **Children pricing** | €1,350 | **Sonia: same price for all ages?** (Needs to confirm) |
    57|| **Payment options** | Credit card, cash, **Bitcoin (USDT)** | Cash, pending WhatsApp bot payments |
    58|
    59|### What This Means for Sonia
    60|
    61|| Learn | Apply to Nexa |
    62||-------|-------------|
    63|| Their price is **€2,800** ($3,100) for residency alone | Sonia's **$1,500** is half their price. This is a HUGE competitive advantage. She can say: *"Misma calidad, mitad de precio."* |
    64|| They offer an **all-in expedition** for €5,397 that includes hotels, meals, helicopter, Iguazú | Sonia could offer a **lower-cost, no-frills version**: no helicopter, no fancy hotels, just her + her car + her network. Price: ~$2,500-$3,000 for the complete experience. |
    65|| They charge **€1,350 for children** | Sonia should decide: same price for families (simpler) or tiered? |
    66|| They accept **Bitcoin/stablecoins** | Sonia doesn't need this yet — cash + bank transfer is sufficient for her volume. |
    67|
    68|---
    69|
    70|## 3. SITE STRUCTURE & PAGES
    71|
    72|| Page | What They Cover | Notes |
    73||------|----------------|-------|
    74|| **Home** | Hero, mission, call-to-action, featured blog posts, email capture | Simple but effective. Slogan: "Where Freedom Meets Future" |
    75|| **Verblijfsvergunning** (Residency) | Full process, requirements, pricing, FAQ | **Direct page.** Clean. Transparent about what's needed. |
    76|| **Onroerend goed** (Real Estate) | Investment benefits, tax info, market data | Very convincing macro-arguments. 7 accordion panels. |
    77|| **Expeditie Paraguay** | 10-day tour itinerary, pricing, FAQ | Strongest page. Ultra-detailed day-by-day. Massive FOMO. |
    78|| **FAQ** | Residency + real estate questions | **Excellent.** Comprehensive. Anticipates objections. |
    79|| **Plan B** | Lead magnet landing page | **"6 reasons why a Plan B outside Europe is a good idea"** |
    80|| **Waarom Paraguay** (Why Paraguay) | Benefits of the country | Strong macro-arguments. 6 benefits listed. |
    81|| **Contact** | Simple contact form | Minimal. No WhatsApp integration visible. |
    82|| **Blog** | 20+ posts, YouTube embeds, frequent updates | **Very active.** Multiple posts per week. Heavy on political content + tax updates. |
    83|| **404 page** | Has a redirect + ebook download offer | Smart — captures leads even on errors. |
    84|
    85|### What They Have That We Don't
    86|
    87|| Feature | Project Paraguay | Nexa Paraguay | Should We Add? |
    88||---------|-----------------|---------------|----------------|
    89|| **Expeditie/group tour** | ✅ 10-day escorted trip | ❌ | 🟡 Sonia could offer informal accompaniment but not a packaged tour |
    90|| **Blog with YouTube** | ✅ 20+ blog posts, YouTube channel with 60+ videos | ✅ 30 posts (generic AI) | 🟢 Match with real content |
    91|| **Lead magnets** | ✅ "Free ebook" on Plan B + "Why Paraguay" | ❌ | ✅ **YES — high priority** |
    92|| **Telegram group** | ✅ Private group for clients | ❌ | 🟡 WhatsApp group might work better for her audience |
    93|| **FAQ page** | ✅ Comprehensive | ❌ (scattered across site) | ✅ **YES — build this** |
    94|| **Rekentool (calculator)** | ✅ Residency visit timing calculator | ❌ (tax calculator planned) | 🟢 Different calculators, both good |
    95|| **Real estate page** | ✅ Dedicated page with Lester's expertise | ❌ (mentioned in services) | 🟢 Commission model means she doesn't need a full RE page |
    96|| **Container transport** | ✅ Shipping household goods | ❌ | ❌ Not relevant for small-format clients |
    97|| **Crypto payments** | ✅ Bitcoin/USDT | ❌ | ❌ Not needed yet |
    98|| **404 page with lead capture** | ✅ Smart | ❌ | ✅ **Easy win — implement** |
    99|| **Delivery policy page** | ✅ Stripe-compliant cancellation/refund policy | ❌ | 🟡 Needed if she processes payments online |
   100|
   101|---
   102|
   103|## 4. CONTENT STRATEGY — WHAT WORKS FOR THEM
   104|
   105|### Blog Content Mix
   106|
   107|| Category | % of Posts | Example Topics |
   108||----------|-----------|----------------|
   109|| **Tax/legal updates** | 30% | Box 3 changes, exit tax, new Paraguay rules |
   110|| **Political/freedom content** | 25% | Thierry Baudet visit, VPRO documentary, Willem Engel, WO III |
   111|| **Community events** | 20% | Expeditions, asados, record-breaking, aftermovies |
   112|| **Practical advice** | 15% | VERBLIJFSVERGUNNING REGELS (most popular post), costs, requirements |
   113|| **Personal stories** | 10% | Jeroen's experiences, client testimonials |
   114|
   115|### What We Can Steal (Ethically)
   116|
   117|| Tactic | How to Adapt for Nexa |
   118||--------|----------------------|
   119|| **Comprehensive blog post on residency rules** | ✅ Sonia's version can be SIMPLER, FRIENDLIER, more personal. Not legalistic — "Sonia explains in 5 minutes how residency works". Add a **visual timeline** (Sonia's approach vs corporate approach). |
   120|| **"Rekentool" (calculator)** | ✅ Build the tax calculator Sonia approved. Later add a residency timing calculator like theirs. |
   121|| **FAQ page** | ✅ Sonia has answered 80+ questions across 3 meetings. Compile them into a clear FAQ on the site. |
   122|| **Lead magnets on every page** | ✅ "10 cosas que nadie te contó sobre Paraguay" — put the email capture on every page. |
   123|| **Testimonials slider** | ✅ They have 12 rotating testimonials. Sonia has 10 clients. Get them to write something. |
   124|| **Ask a question → download** pattern | ✅ Every page has: Email → download → nurture sequence. Copy this exactly. |
   125|
   126|### What NOT to Copy
   127|
   128|| Tactic | Why Avoid It |
   129||--------|-------------|
   130|| **Political/fear-based messaging** | Sonia is apolitical. Her brand is "relax, I can help you" — not "flee before it's too late." |
   131|| **"Plan B" / "Crisis" framing** | Sonia should position as "A better life" not "An escape route." Positive framing. |
   132|| **Paid expedition model** | Too complex for Sonia. She could offer a "Day 1 companion" experience but not a 10-day group tour. |
   133|| **VPRO documentary controversy** | Project Paraguay is deeply embedded in the "Wakker in Paraguay" controversy. Sonia should stay far from this. |
   134|| **Heavy tax/legal content** | Project Paraguay has Toine Manders (tax lawyer). Sonia doesn't. Her content should be **practical and personal**, not technical. |
   135|
   136|---
   137|
   138|## 5. WHAT SONIA HAS THAT PROJECT PARAGUAY DOESN'T
   139|
   140|| Sonia's Advantage | Why It Matters |
   141||-------------------|----------------|
   142|| **$1,500 vs €2,800** | **47% cheaper.** This is massive. Put this front and center. |
   143|| **Personal story + reinvention narrative** | Project Paraguay's face is Jeroen Pols (political commentator). Sonia is a mother who reinvented herself. Massively more relatable. |
   144|| **Family focus** | Project Paraguay targets "freedom-seekers" and investors. Sonia targets families, retirees, and people who want a peaceful life. |
   145|| **Post-residency support ($200/day)** | Project Paraguay offers one-time service. Sonia accompanies for up to a year. |
   146|| **Commission-free property help** | Project Paraguay has a Century 21 agent charging commissions to BOTH sides. Sonia's model is "free to client, paid by seller." |
   147|| **Neighborhood intelligence** | **1,485 places with GPS** – no competitor has this. Sonia can hand a client a map of their neighborhood with schools, hospitals, supermarkets, and restaurants. |
   148|| **Real Paraguayan (not expat)** | Sonia LIVED abroad and returned. She's not a Dutch expat living in Paraguay — she's Paraguayan who understands both worlds. |
   149|| **Luana (bilingual daughter)** | Young, fresh face. Can connect with younger clients. Speaks Dutch natively. |
   150|| **WhatsApp bot (soon)** | Automation advantage. Project Paraguay uses Telegram + email. WhatsApp is the dominant platform. |
   151|| **Huaste/Spanish bilingual** | Can serve Latin American and European clients. |
   152|| **Lower cost of operations** | No office, no employees, no expedition logistics. Her overhead is essentially zero. |
   153|
   154|---
   155|
   156|## 6. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS
   157|
   158|### Steal These Immediately (Free, Fast)
   159|
   160|| Action | From Project Paraguay | Effort |
   161||--------|---------------------|--------|
   162|| **Add FAQ page** | They have a comprehensive FAQ on residency + real estate | 1 hour to compile from Sonia's 80+ answered questions |
   163|| **Email capture on every page** | Every page has "Email → download" | 1 hour of dev work |
   164|| **Lead magnet landing page** | "6 reasons for a Plan B" / "Why Paraguay" | 1 hour to write + set up form |
   165|| **Testimonials slider** | 12 rotating testimonials on the residency page | 30 min (waiting on Sonia's clients) |
   166|| **Add pricing to the residency page** | Transparent €2,800 shown directly | 15 min (Sonia confirmed $1,500 OK) |
   167|| **404 page with email capture** | Redirects to ebook download | 2 min to set up |
   168|
   169|### Adapt In The Next Sprint
   170|
   171|| Action | Why | Effort |
   172||--------|-----|--------|
   173|| **Blog post: "How residency works in Paraguay in 2026"** | Their most popular post. Ours can be simpler and more personal. | 2 hours |
   174|| **Visual timeline of the residency process** | They explain it in text. Sonia can do a timeline: Day 1 → Week 2 → Month 2 → Month 6 | 1 hour |
   175|| **"Why Sonia" page** | Direct comparison: Project Paraguay vs Nexa. Free comparison without naming names. | 2 hours |
   176|| **Rekentool adaptation** | Their calculator is for residency timing. Sonia's is for taxes. Both are calculators. | 4 hours (planned anyway) |
   177|
   178|### Don't Bother With
   179|
   180|| Not Needed | Why |
   181||-----------|-----|
   182|| YouTube channel | Sonia doesn't want to be a content creator. Blog + testimonials are enough. |
   183|| Telegram group | WhatsApp is her platform. |
   184|| Paid expeditions | Too complex, too expensive. She does NOT have the bandwidth for group tours. |
   185|| Container transport | Not relevant. Her clients bring luggage, not households. |
   186|| Political content | Would alienate her target audience (families, retirees). |
   187|
   188|---
   189|
   190|## 7. DIRECT COMPARISON TABLE
   191|
   192|| Dimension | Project Paraguay | Nexa Paraguay (Sonia) |
   193||-----------|-----------------|----------------------|
   194|| **Language** | Dutch (NL) | Spanish + will localize to ES/EN/NL/DE |
   195|| **Price** | €2,800 (~$3,100) | **$1,500** |
   196|| **Target** | Freedom-seekers, investors, "Plan B" | Families, retirees, peaceful life-seekers |
   197|| **Tone** | Political, freedom-focused | Personal, warm, "relax" |
   198|| **Location** | Hohenau (rural, south) | Asunción/Casa Weiss (urban, central) |
   199|| **Property focus** | Yes (Century 21 affiliate, Lester) | Limited (commission-based, no dedicated agent) |
   200|| **Group tours** | ✅ Expedities (€3,397-€5,397) | ❌ |
   201|| **Post-residency** | Limited (after sales service) | ✅ Strong ($200/day, up to 1 year) |

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Propuesta Completa para Sonia

> **Propósito:** Que Sonia tenga TODAS las opciones, ideas y planes en un solo lugar para elegir, validar y mejorar.
> **Documento vivo** — lo actualizamos después de cada reunión.
> **Formato:** Opciones con su contexto, costo, esfuerzo y recomendación. Sonia marca lo que quiere.
> **Documentos relacionados:** `docs/meetings/meeting-sonia-questions-final.md`, `docs/SITE_CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/meetings/meeting-report-may-11.md`, `docs/09-market-intelligence/competitor-analysis-projectparaguay.md`

---

## CÓMO LEER ESTE DOCUMENTO

Cada propuesta tiene esta estructura:

| Campo | Qué significa |
|-------|--------------|
| **Propuesta** | Qué estamos proponiendo |
| **Contexto** | Por qué existe esta opción |
| **Opción A / B / C** | Las alternativas que Sonia puede elegir |
| **Recomendación** | Nuestra sugerencia (marcada con ✅) |
| **Esfuerzo** | Horas de trabajo para implementar |
| **Impacto** | Bajo / Medio / Alto en el negocio |
| **Riesgo** | Qué podría salir mal |
| **Dependencia** | Qué necesitamos de Sonia para arrancar |
| **Sonia decide** | ☐ Espacio para que Sonia marque su elección |

---

## 1. PRECIO DEL SERVICIO PRINCIPAL

### 1.1 — Precio de Residencia

**Contexto:** Confirmaste $1,500. Project Paraguay cobra €2,800 (~$3,100). Competidores locales cobran $2,500-$3,500. Tenemos que decidir cómo presentar este precio en el sitio.

| Opción | Descripción | Ejemplo | Recomendación |
|--------|-------------|---------|---------------|
| **A — Precio fijo único** | $1,500 para todos. Simple, transparente, poderoso. | "Residencia completa: **$1,500** — precio único, sin sorpresas" | ✅ RECOMENDADO |
| **B — Precio variable** | $1,500 base + extras según complejidad (hijos, documentos extra, etc.) | "Desde $1,500. Cotización personalizada" | 🟡 Si la complejidad realmente varía |
| **C — Precio con descuento promocional** | $1,500 como "lanzamiento" para captar clientes rápido | "Lanzamiento: $1,500 (precio regular $2,500)" | ❌ No — ella ya tiene 10 clientes, no es nuevo negocio |

**Sonia decide:** ☐ A ☐ B ☐ C

### 1.2 — Precio para niños/familia

**Contexto:** Project Paraguay cobra €1,350 por hijo menor de 14 años. Vos dijiste "mismo precio para todos" en la reunión de May 11, pero no confirmaste si los hijos cuestan menos.

| Opción | Descripción | Ejemplo |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| **A — Mismo precio** | Cada adulto paga $1,500. Hijos incluidos sin costo extra. | "Residencia individual: $1,500. Cónyuge e hijos sin costo adicional." |
| **B — Precio reducido por hijo** | Hijos pagan menos (ej: $750 c/u). | "Hijos: $750 cada uno (50% descuento)" |
| **C — Precio por familia** | Paquete familiar: $X para toda la familia. | "Paquete familiar (hasta 4 personas): $2,500" |

**Sonia decide:** ☐ A ☐ B ☐ C ☐ Otra: _______________

### 1.3 — Post-residencia: $200/día

**Contexto:** Confirmaste $200/día con Carlos. Necesitamos definir los bordes.

| Opción | Descripción |
|--------|-------------|
| **A — Día completo (8h)** | $200 por día completo de acompañamiento. Viaje + tiempo. |
| **B — Medio día (4h)** | $100 por medio día. Para trámites rápidos. |
| **C — Precio por tarea** | Banco: $XX. Colegios: $XX. Compra de auto: $XX. |

**Recomendación:** Opción A + B (día completo y medio día). Simple y flexible.

**Sonia decide:** ☐ A ☐ B ☐ A+B ☐ C

### 1.4 — Comisiones: ¿mostrar o no?

**Contexto:** Ganás 2.75% en propiedades. En vehículos y electrodomésticos también ganás comisión. Esto es GRATIS para el cliente pero no sabemos cuánto ganás exactamente.

| Opción | Descripción |
|--------|-------------|
| **A — Mostrar como "sin costo para vos"** | "Propiedades: sin costo para vos. Nos pagamos con comisión del vendedor." |
| **B — Mostrar el % de comisión** | "2.75% de comisión (pagado por el vendedor, no por vos)" |
| **C — No mencionar comisiones en el sitio** | Solo decirlo en persona. |

**Recomendación:** Opción A. Es un diferenciador enorme frente a competidores que cobran al comprador.

**Sonia decide:** ☐ A ☐ B ☐ C

### 1.5 — Cotizaciones personalizadas

**Contexto:** Para servicios que no tienen precio fijo (ej: investigación de colegios en 3 ciudades, acompañamiento médico complejo).

| Opción | Descripción |
|--------|-------------|
| **A — Botón "Solicitar cotización"** | El cliente llena un formulario corto → Sonia responde por WhatsApp |
| **B — Precio por hora** | $XX/hora para cualquier servicio no listado |
| **C — Sin cotizaciones en el sitio** | "Todo se coordina por WhatsApp" |

**Recomendación:** Opción A. El botón de cotización permite capturar leads sin comprometer un precio.

**Sonia decide:** ☐ A ☐ B ☐ C

## The Proposal Document — What's Inside

**`docs/propuesta-completa-sonia.md`** — a single document with EVERYTHING Sonia needs to choose from.

### 10 Sections, 30+ Decisions with Options

| Section | What It Covers | Options |
|---------|---------------|---------|
| **1. Pricing** | 5 decisions: base price, kids, post-residency, commissions, quotes | A/B/C each with recommendation |
| **2. Site Structure** | Pages (keep/delete/create), navigation (standard vs minimal), lead magnets (5 options) | 12 possible new pages, she picks |
| **3. Content** | Voice per section, slogan final choice, personal story borrador | 3 voice options, 4 slogan variants |
| **4. New Features** | Tax calculator, neighborhood map, WhatsApp bot | She decides yes/no per feature |
| **5. Project Paraguay** | 6 things to STEAL, 4 to ADAPT, 6 to AVOID | All with justifications |
| **6. Photos & Assets** | 6 things we need from Sonia with urgency levels | Checklist |
| **7. Action Plan** | 3 phases: immediate (2h), post-meeting (11h), ongoing | Timeline |
| **8. Meeting Checklist** | 25 questions to resolve, sorted 🔴 🟡 🟢 | Ready for the call |
| **9. Her Ideas** | Blank space for Sonia to add her own | Open |
| **10. Approval** | Signature block for decisions | |

### Design Philosophy

Every proposal follows the same structure:
```
Propuesta → Contexto → Opción A / B / C → Recomendación → 
Esfuerzo → Impacto → Riesgo → Dependencia → [Sonia decide: ☐]
```

This way Sonia can:
1. Read the context
2. See the options clearly
3. Know our recommendation
4. Mark what she wants with a checkbox
5. Add her own notes

### What's Already in the Git Repo

All committed and pushed to `main` — `docs/propuesta-completa-sonia.md` is the single source for the next meeting.

Review the conversation above and update two things:

**Memory**: who the user is. Did the user reveal persona, desires, preferences, personal details, or expectations about how you should behave? Save facts about the user and durable preferences with the memory tool.

**Skills**: how to do this class of task. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the skill library: CLASS-LEVEL skills with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries.

Signals that warrant a skill update (any one is enough):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, verbosity, or approach. Frustration is a FIRST-CLASS skill signal, not just a memory signal. 'stop doing X', 'don't format like this', 'I hate when you Y' — embed the lesson in the skill that governs that task so the next session starts fixed.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, or debugging path emerged.
  • A skill that was loaded or consulted turned out wrong, missing, or outdated — patch it now.

Preference order for skills — pick the earliest that fits:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Check what skills were loaded via /skill-name or skill_view in the conversation. If one of them covers the learning, PATCH it first. It was in play; it's the right place.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (skills_list + skill_view to find the right one). Patch it.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella via skill_manage action=write_file. Three kinds: `references/<topic>.md` for session-specific detail OR condensed knowledge banks (quoted research, API docs excerpts, domain notes) written concise and task-focused; `templates/<name>.<ext>` for starter files meant to be copied and modified; `scripts/<name>.<ext>` for statically re-runnable actions (verification, fixture generators, probes). Add a one-line pointer in SKILL.md so future agents find them.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA when nothing exists. Name at the class level — NOT a PR number, error string, codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y' session artifact. If the name only fits today's task, fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding: when the user complains about how you handled a task, update the skill that governs that task — memory alone isn't enough. Memory says 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills say 'how to do this class of task for this user'. Both should carry user-preference lessons when relevant.

If you notice overlapping existing skills, mention it — the background curator handles consolidation.

Do NOT capture as skills (these become persistent self-imposed constraints that bite you later when the environment changes):
  • Environment-dependent failures: missing binaries, fresh-install errors, post-migration path mismatches, 'command not found', unconfigured credentials, uninstalled packages. The user can fix these — they are not durable rules.
  • Negative claims about tools or features ('browser tools do not work', 'X tool is broken', 'cannot use Y from execute_code'). These harden into refusals the agent cites against itself for months after the actual problem was fixed.
  • Session-specific transient errors that resolved before the conversation ended. If retrying worked, the lesson is the retry pattern, not the original failure.
  • One-off task narratives. A user asking 'summarize today's market' or 'analyze this PR' is not a class of work that warrants a skill.

If a tool failed because of setup state, capture the FIX (install command, config step, env var to set) under an existing setup or troubleshooting skill — never 'this tool does not work' as a standalone constraint.

Act on whichever of the two dimensions has real signal. If genuinely nothing stands out on either, say 'Nothing to save.' and stop — but don't reach for that conclusion as a default.
Let me review the conversation for patterns, corrections, and learnings.

# Nexa Content Generation & Management Skill

Goal: Generate SEO-optimized blog articles in all 4 locales, register them in the site's content JSON, and manage the large JSON-driven content files across locales.

## Workflow

1. **Research topic** — Use multi-source-research skill for keyword/competitor analysis
2. **Brief** — 1 brief per persona/locale with target keyword, H2 outline, tone
3. **Generate** — 1 subagent per locale, max 4 articles each
4. **Register** — Build blog/posts JSON entries, commit
5. **Verify** — Check each locale has the article, build + deploy

## Content File Structure

```
content/blog/
├── posts.json          # Spanish (default)
├── posts-en.json       # English
├── posts-nl.json       # Dutch
└── posts-de.json       # German
```

Each file has format: `{ "posts": [ { "slug", "title", "excerpt", "date", "body", "tags" } ] }`

## Article Format

Body is inline text in the JSON `body` field. Newlines as `\n`. No MDX files needed — content lives in posts.json directly.

## Editing Large JSON Content Files

The `content/{locale}.json` files are large (200KB-600KB, [REDACTED_PHONE] lines). The `patch` tool often fails on these due to:
- **Escape-drift**: quotes inside old_string/new_string get spurious backslashes
- **Pagination warnings**: pre-read file warnings trigger false positives
- **Subagent race conditions**: sibling agents may modify files without reading first

**Preferred approach: Use `execute_code` with Python's json module.**

```python
import json
with open('/root/nexa-paraguay/content/es.json') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# Make changes using dict/list operations
data['home']['hero']['trustBadges'] = ["8–12 semanas", "Precio fijo"]
del data['home']['beneluxDesk']

with open('/root/nexa-paraguay/content/es.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
```

This works for all locales. Batch changes across `es`, `en`, `nl`, `de` in one script.

**When the files have different structures** (en.json is flat keys, es.json is nested), check with `if 'home' in data:` before accessing nested keys.

## Key Structural Differences Between Locales

| File | Structure | Key nesting |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| `content/es.json` | Nested | `home.hero.headline`, `aboutPage.story`, `faqPage` |
| `content/nl.json` | Nested | Same as es.json |
| `content/de.json` | Nested | Same as es.json |
| `content/en.json` | Mixed flat + nested | About 40% of content is flat root-level keys |
| `content/blog/posts-{locale}.json` | Blog-specific | `{ "posts": [ { "slug", "title", "body" } ] }` |

The en.json structure diverged because it was created from a different workflow. Always verify structure before batch-editing.

## Applying Client Content Feedback (multi-locale)

When client provides rewritten copy for homepage/about/services sections:

1. Identify the exact content paths in JSON (e.g. `home.hero.headline`, `aboutPage.story.paragraphs`)
2. For each change, apply to all 4 locale files in a single Python script
3. Verify: `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); json.load(open('content/en.json')); json.load(open('content/nl.json')); json.load(open('content/de.json'))"` — all must pass
4. Build with `rm -rf .next && npx next build` — watch for route count and PPR status
5. Commit with a descriptive msg listing the actual content changes

## Restoring Corrupted JSON From Git

If patch tool double-escapes quotes or creates invalid JSON:

```bash
git checkout -- content/es.json
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('RESTORED')"
```

Then reapply changes via Python json module, NOT patch tool.

## Content Voice Guidelines (Sonia / Nexa Paraguay)

Sonia's brand is "acompañamiento de cerca" — close guidance over up to a year. Content should:

- Use **first person** for the founder story (Sonia lived in the Netherlands, emigrated herself)
- Avoid third-person "la fundadora" or "nuestra fundadora" — write "Viví en los Países Bajos"
- Lead with empathy: "sé lo que se siente llegar a un país y no tener nadie que te guíe"
- Focus on the **long-term accompaniment** (up to 1 year), not transactional document processing
- Reject fabricated stats — use honest claims only (process duration, fixed price, personal support)
- The hero should say: "Viví el proceso con alguien que ya lo vivió" or similar personal framing

## Registration

After generating content, update the correct `posts-{locale}.json` file(s) by appending entries. Verify with:
```
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('content/blog/posts-en.json')); print(len(d.get('posts',[])))"
```

## Pitfalls

- **Subagent race conditions**: A sibling agent may modify SectionsRenderer.tsx or content JSON files while you're also editing them. Read the file first with `read_file` before `patch`.
- **en.json has different structure**: It uses flat keys at the root level (`hero`, `process`, `trust` are top-level objects, not nested under `home`). Always verify structure before batch editing. Check with `if 'home' in data:` before accessing nested keys.
- **patch on Spanish content causes escape-drift**: Spanish text has quotes, accents, and special chars (`"`, `'`, `¿`, `¡`, `áéíóúñ`). The patch tool often double-escapes these (`\\\"`, `\\\\\"`) when they appear inside old_string/new_string. This corrupts JSON. **For any file with Spanish content, skip patch and use `execute_code` with Python's json module.** When patch does corrupt the file, restore with `git checkout -- <file>` and retry via Python.
- **Verify all 4 locales after batch edit**: Content structures differ. After a batch script, run: `python3 -c "import json; [json.load(open(f'content/{l}.json')) for l in ['es','en','nl','de']]; print('ALL VALID')\"` to catch structural mismatches.
- **Docker build fails with "Couldn't find any pages or app directory"**: Check `.dockerignore`. The file may contain `src/app` which excludes the entire app directory from the Docker context. If it exists, remove that line and rebuild.
- **Content updates may not reflect after deploy**: The Docker build may cache the `.next` build output. Use `docker build --no-cache` and `docker service update --force` when content changes don't appear.
- **PPR removed from nexa site**: `cacheComponents: true` was removed from `next.config.js`. Suspense wrappers were stripped from page files. All routes now render as `● Static` (fully pre-rendered HTML). The PPR-era behavior (empty `<main>` in curl, content only visible via JS) no longer applies.
- **Client feedback often targets 3-5% of content**: The rest stays. Python dict operations let you surgically edit without touching thousands of unchanged lines.
- **Feedback section is client-side only**: The `FeedbackSection` component uses localStorage — no backend needed for MVP. Export format is JSON array of `{name, message, date}` objects.

## See Also

- `studio-management/references/multi-locale-client-feedback-cycle.md` (in the studio-management skill) — detailed guide for handling client content feedback across locales, including cascading fixes for PPR, section references, and component wiring.
- **Client feedback often targets 3-5% of content**: The rest stays. Python dict operations let you surgically edit without touching thousands of unchanged lines.
- **Feedback section is client-side only**: The `FeedbackSection` component uses localStorage — no backend needed for MVP. Export format is JSON array of `{name, message, date}` objects.
